Description: Estimating state-space models using the N4SID method in the System Identification Tool GUI produces an error when you: Set frequency-domain data as Working Data. Frequency domain data is either the Fourier transform of input and output time-domain signals (IDDATA object with Domain = 'Frequency') or frequency response data (IDFRD object). Click the Order Selection button in the Linear Parametric Models dialog box. You can also specify this option by entering a range of values in the Orders field.
Workaround: Download and install the patch that replaces the file "n4sid_f.m":1. (Optional) If you do not know your MATLAB root directory, type "matlabroot" at the MATLAB prompt.2. Exit MATLAB software.3. Download the attached file n4sid_f.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/ident/ident/, where <matlabroot> is your MATLAB root directory.4. Start MATLAB.5. Type the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Import of numerical DBF columns in function shaperead may not be sufficiently robust
Description: When function shaperead  imports the .dbf ("xBase") component of a shapefile data set, it's possible for the numerical values to be garbled. This happens when there's a numerically-valued column with tightly packed digits. This problem does not occur with shapefiles exported from the Mapping Toolbox shapewrite  function, nor with shapefiles written by other software products. However, because the problem is known to exist for DBF files in general -- it just hasn't been seen in the DBF component of a shapefile -- the way in which shaperead  imports numerical shapefile attributes may need to be made more robust.
Workaround: To ensure more robust DBF import, replace the dbfinfo.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/private/dbfinfo.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: dbfinfo.m.oldCopy the version of dbfinfo.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Simulink may crash when using a Model block in a function-call subsystem that is called asynchronously
Description: Simulink may crash when simulating a model with: A Model block that Has the Simulation mode  set to Normal Is in a function-call subsystem   A function-call initiator that is driving the function-call subsystem at an asynchronous rate  The stack trace that is produced may contain the following frames: Stack Trace: [0] libmwsimulink.so:slSetupTIDMapsForNormalMode(slBlockDiagram_tag const*, slBlock const*)(0x2aaad8a1ff60, 0x407f12d0, 0x081c76d0 "ˇă????*", 0) + 343 bytes [1] libmwsimulink.so:sleLinkBlockDiagramsTopDownRecursively(slModel*)(0x10000081c76d0, 0x081c76d0 "ˇă????*", 0x400000000, 0x081c7ca0) + 402 bytes [2] libmwsimulink.so:sleInitModelConditions(slModel*, mxArray_tag const*, bool, bool)(0xffffffff00000004, 0, 0, 0) + 4399 bytes [3] libmwsimulink.so:DoRTWGen(RTWInfo*, mxArray_tag*, int, mxArray_tag**)(0x2aaad8a1ff60, 0x00706f74, 0, 0) + 1588 bytes

Summary: External simulation, using xPC target, attempts to check out Fixed Point Toolbox license and receives an error if license is not available, even when the model does not use fixed-point values
Description: xPC targets upload all data with type double . However, external simulations require data be uploaded to a Simulink model in the data type expected by the model. For an xPC target, Simulink software converts to the expected data type by using a fi  object. To create a fi  object, MATLAB requires a Fixed Point Toolbox license. Therefore, if the license is not available, an error occurs, whether or not the model contains fixed-point values.
Workaround: Copy the attached file to Matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/ directory.

Summary: Calling xmlcomp.register clears some global data
Description: You must call the MATLAB command xmlcomp.register to enable XML Comparison features of the MATLAB Report Generator. Within this command a call to "javaaddpath" is made. This clears some types of global data, such as persistent variables, MATLAB global variables and MATLAB Editor breakpoints. The call to "javaaddpath" occurs just once per MATLAB session, regardless of how many times xmlcomp.register is called.
Workaround: To avoid losing any global data, ensure that you make the call to xmlcomp.register at the start of each MATLAB session. You can do this manually (via a MATLAB Desktop shortcut, for example), or automatically at the start of every session by editing matlabrc.m.

Summary: Sobol sets cannot be used in a compiled application
Description: If you compile an application that uses the sobolset  function to create a sobol set, you get an error when it is run. This is because the sobolset object loads in data from a mat file which is not automatically found by the compiler's dependency analysis.
Workaround: Include the required file manually in the mcc command when you compile the application. The path to the file from the root of your MATLAB installation is the following: toolbox\stats\@sobolset\private\DirectionInit.mat

Summary: If Target Preferences block specifies more than one custom source file, software generates error during code generation
Description: If the Target Preferences (TP) block in your model specifies more than one custom source file, the software generates the following error message when you generate code from the model: Error using ==> feval Error using ==> i_exitHookpoint at 80 Error while constructing the project: Error using ==> strfind Input strings must have one row.The build process will terminate as a result. ? ?
Workaround: To install the patch:Using Windows Explorer, locate the MATLAB root folder on your system. For example, locate C:\Program Files\MATLAB\Locate the following files in your MATLAB root folder and create backup copies of them:<matlabroot>\toolbox\shared\idelinks\pjtgenerator\@LinkTgtPkg\@Board\getSourceFiles.p<matlabroot>\toolbox\shared\idelinks\pjtgenerator\tgtpref\TgtPref.mDownload and open the patch .zip file.Inside the patch zip file, double-click the the patch_506641_*.exe file for your version of MATLAB. For example, if you are using the R2007b version of MATLAB, double click patch_506641_R2007b.exe.In the WinZip Self-Extractor that opens, verify that Unzip to folder is set to the MATLAB root folder. For example: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\Click the Unzip button. This action installs the patch files.To verify that the patch works:Open MATLAB.In the MATLAB Command Window, enter rehash toolboxresetGenerate code for a model with a Target Preferences block that specifies two or more custom files.?

Summary: MATLAB adds duplicate entries to actxlicense.m when user creates a licensed ActiveX control.
Description: Duplicate entries are added to actxlicense.m  when user creates a licensed ActiveX control.
Workaround: None.

Summary: Reading some ICC profiles using iccread can result in an "Invalid size" error
Description: Using iccread  to read some ICC profiles can result in an "Invalid size" error. Reading DeviceLink profiles containing empty device manufacture strings, for example, can sometimes result in this error.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the versions of the iccread function in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make backup copies of the version of this function in the following directory:matlabroot\toolbox\images\colorspaces\iccread.m where matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing files, adding "old" to the file extension, iccread.m.old.Download the attached file, extract iccread.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.?

Summary: When opening the Automatic Orange Tester the following error appears: 'The value must be a float or an integer'
Description: When opening a .tgf  file in the Automatic Orange Tester, one or more errors are displayed. The error message is the following: The value must be a float or an integer, 'min' or 'max'. Use '..' for a range. e.g : '1.9' or '2.0.4.0' for float.
Workaround: To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\AutomaticOrangeTester\binCreate a backup of the existing files in the folder aboveDownload the file 'AOT-g506157.zip' in attachmentExtract the zip file in %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\AutomaticOrangeTester\binTo workaround this issue on a UNIX system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_COMMON/AutomaticOrangeTester/binCreate a backup of the existing files in the folder aboveDownload the file 'AOT-g506157.zip' in attachmentExtract the zip file in $POLYSPACE_COMMON/AutomaticOrangeTester/bin?

Summary: Switch block might cause MATLAB to crash when output port is set to non-numeric data types
Description: A Simulink model might crash if the following conditions are met: A Switch block is in the model. The data type of the Switch block's output port is back propagated from downstream blocks. The Switch block's output port is set to be any non-numeric data type.
Workaround: Explicitly set the Switch block's input data ports and output port to be the same non-numeric data types.

Summary: Unable to create objects for Keithley or VXI Technology adaptors
Description: These instructions on MATLAB Central allow you to add support for the deprecated Keithley and VXI Technology adaptors: For Keithley: https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/fileexchange/21668 For VXI Technology: https://www.mathworks.com/matlabcentral/fileexchange/21671 However, you will still get an error when you try to create an analoginput, analogoutput or digitalio object: ai = analoginput('keithley', 0);  ??? Error using ==> privateCheckObsoleteAdaptor at 24The Keithley adaptor ('keithley')is no longer part of Data Acquisition Toolbox. It is now available on the MATLAB Central File Exchange. Please see Solution 1-37TKH4 for details.Error in ==> daqgate at 19feval(varargin{:});Error in ==> analoginput.analoginput at 118daqgate('privateCheckObsoleteAdaptor', className,adaptor);
Workaround: To work around this problem follow these steps:Quit MATLAB.Find the following file: $MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq\private\privateCheckObsoleteAdaptor.m (where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root installation directory obtained by issuing: matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the file to privateCheckObsoleteAdaptor.m.OLD.Download and extract the attached replacement file to the same directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Bus Selector and Bus Assignment blocks connected to Shadow Inport blocks can malfunction
Description: A Bus Selector or Bus Assignment block that is connected to a Shadow Inport block may fail if: The shadow Inport block is in a subsystem of any kind. A valid bus object is specified in the Shadow Inport block parameter dialog. The subsystem input port corresponding to the Shadow Inport block is unconnected.  In this situation, a downstream Bus Selector or Bus Assignment block will omit the bus hierarchy from the block parameter dialog.

Summary: Error message "must set output port width" incorrectly displayed when S-function with inherited or dynamic output port width uses S-function APIs to set asynchronous task timer
Description: S-function APIs such as ssSetTimeSource  require port width information in order to get or set attributes for each element of a specified port. When called by an S-function for which the port width is not explicitly set but rather inherited or dynamically sized, these APIs should assume that all elements of the port share the same attribute settings and proceed. However, in the case of asynchronous function-call S-functions, the software incorrectly issues a message similar to the following: S-function 'name ' in 'block ' must set output port 'n ' width using ssSetOutputPortWidth before calling macro ssSetTimeSource.

Summary: Digital Filter Design block Convert Structure dialog box broken
Description: The Convert Structure  dialog available from the Digital Filter Design block Edit  menu does not work. When you select a filter structure from this dialog box, the filter structure implemented by the block does not change.

Summary: Java exception on 64-bit Macintosh when saving a figure to a different file format
Description: Changing the file format when saving a figure generates a Java exception on 64 bit Macintosh machines. This issue can happen when using the MATLAB uiputfile  function and changing the file filter after selecting a file. The exception stack trace looks like the following: object is not an instance of declaring classjava.lang.IllegalArgumentException: object is not an instance of declaring classat sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:39)at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:25)at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:585)at com.mathworks.mwswing.MJFileChooser.getFileName(MJFileChooser.java:136)at com.mathworks.mwswing.MJFileChooser.access$000(MJFileChooser.java:40)at com.mathworks.mwswing.MJFileChooser$FilterChangedListener.updateSelectedFileName(MJFileChooser.java:107)at com.mathworks.mwswing.MJFileChooser$FilterChangedListener.propertyChange(MJFileChooser.java:102)at java.beans.PropertyChangeSupport.firePropertyChange(PropertyChangeSupport.java:333)at java.beans.PropertyChangeSupport.firePropertyChange(PropertyChangeSupport.java:270)at java.awt.Component.firePropertyChange(Component.java:7277)at javax.swing.JFileChooser.setFileFilter(JFileChooser.java:1446)at apple.laf.CUIAquaFileChooser$FilterComboBoxAction.actionPerformed(CUIAquaFileChooser.java:1333)at javax.swing.JComboBox.fireActionEvent(JComboBox.java:1197)at javax.swing.JComboBox.setSelectedItem(JComboBox.java:561)at javax.swing.JComboBox.setSelectedIndex(JComboBox.java:597)

Summary: The documentation for the compounding input arguments for zero2pyld and plyd2zero is confusing.
Description: While the descriptions for the Compounding , Basis , and OutputCompounding  input arguments are correct, the argument name OutputCompounding  is misleading because this argument actually applies to the compounding applied to the input rates. Note that the examples for zero2pyld  and plyd2zero  incorrectly use the Compounding  and OutputCompounding  arguments. Finally, also note that the syntax for zero2pyld  and plyd2zero  is inconsistent with zero2fwd  and fwd2zero .
Workaround: The workaround is for the user to have a greater awareness of what the input arguments for zero2pyld and plyd2zero actually represent.

Summary: Single colon indexing may give incorrect results when used in conjunction with assignment.
Description: Single colon indexing may give incorrect results when used in a MATLAB file function and in conjunction with assignment. The following short program illustrates the behavior:    function bug  s(1) = 1;  t = s(:);  s(1) = 2;  t              % returns 2, but should be 1  t = s(:);  t(1) = 3;  s              % returns 3, but should be 2
Workaround: Turn the JIT off using by entering the command   feature accel off

Summary: Working in the Design Configuration Wizard GUI may crash MATLAB?
Description: MATLAB? may crash when you work in the Design Configuration Wizard GUI. You launch the wizard by clicking the Tune Blocks  buttons within a Simulink Compensator Design Task in the Control and Estimation Tools Manager.
Workaround: Install the patch described in following Bug Report: https://www.mathworks.com/support/bugreports/details.html?rp=494721Extract the attached SCDPatch.zip file into the MATLAB? root directory and overwrite the original file.Restart MATLAB?.In the MATLAB? command window, type the following command: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Bloomberg realtime functionality does not process data events.
Description: When registering callbacks to process Bloomberg realtime data events, the callbacks never run and there are no error messages to indicate that there is a problem. For example, b = bloomberg;d = fetch(b,'ABC US Equity','REALTIME',{'Last_Trade','Bid','Ask','Volume','VWAP'},'showtrades');  appears to register the callback 'showtrades' but no data events process.

Summary: SUBS returns an incorrect expression if the substituting variable does not occur in the expression
Description: The code syms x y zsubs(x + y, z, 1) incorrectly substitutes the constant 1 with z in x+y, and returns x*z + y*z
Workaround: To avoid incorrect substitution, pass a trailing 0 to the subs commandsubs(x + y, z, 1, 0)

Summary: Playing a variable-rate video file (such as ASF or WMV) using the From Mulitmedia File block causes only the first frame to be displayed.
Description: If you use the From Multimedia File block to read a variable-rate video, the rate is incorrectly set to be 0 frames per second (FPS) instead of the default rate (1/30). This results in only the first frame of the video being displayed. ?
Workaround: Follow either one of these steps to work around this issue.1. Set the rate of the From Multimedia File block manually.or,1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Save the file contained in the attached zip file in the following location:MATLABROOT/toolbox/dspblks/dspmasks/dspmmfileinfo.m, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine.3. Restart MATLAB.4. Issue the following command:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: Changing certain properties of the axes, legend, or legend text objects resets the legend icons with default property values
Description: This issue occurs when you modify icons on a legend and subsequently modify certain properties of either the axes, legend, or the legend text objects. The following code demonstrates the issue. ax = axes;plot(ax,rand(3));[hLeg,objs,outH,outM] = legend(ax, 'line1', 'line2', 'line3');hIcons = objs(4:2:8); % handles to legend iconshText = objs(1:3);    % handles to legend text items Notice that the colors of the legend icons correspond to the lineseries objects in the axes. Now set the Color  property of the legend icons to yellow . set(hIcons,'Color','yellow') Increase the font size of the legend text objects set(hText,'FontSize',15) Notice that the colors of the legend icons get restored to match the lineseries objects in the axes.
Workaround: Modify the legend icons last, after setting other objects' properties, to ensure that their properties are not reset as a side effect of modifying the axes, legend, or legend text objects.

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox functions gcrmabackadj, gcrma, affygcrma return outputs with smaller dynamic ranges when the GSBCORR property is set to TRUE.
Description: Bioinformatics Toolbox functions gcrmabackadj , gcrma,  and affygcrma  return outputs with smaller dynamic ranges when the GSBCORR  property is set to TRUE . This is caused by a bug in the implementation of gene specific binding (GSB) correction in the gcrmabackadj  function. GSB correction can be turned on or off by setting the GSBCORR  property.
Workaround: Download the attached? file.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\gcrmabackadj.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\gcrmabackadj.m.old,where $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray.Restart MATLAB.Run the command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Unable to delete a trigger port copied from the top-level of a Simulink function-call subsystem inside Stateflow
Description: Simulink function-call subsystems used in Stateflow charts contain a trigger port at the top-level. This trigger port should not be deleted. However, if you copy the trigger port to a different location, Stateflow incorrectly prevents you from deleting the copy and generates the following error message:  Please do not attempt to delete function-call trigger ports in function-call subsystems contained inside a Stateflow chart.
Workaround: Follow these steps: Select the copied block in the Simulink Editor.Run the following command on the MATLAB prompt: >> set_param(gcbh, 'PreDeleteFcn', '');You can now delete the copied trigger port.

Summary: MATLAB Student version installer fails to launch on Windows.
Description: When launching the Student installer the following error is produced: There was an unexpected exception: C:\Users\myuser\Desktop\MatLab\win32\win32\bin\win32\mwinstall.dll: The application has failed to start because its side-by-side configuration is incorrect. Please see the application event log for more detail This error occurs because the MathWorks installer is unable to locate the Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable libraries.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, you can install the libraries yourself, using the following procedure:Navigate to the following folder:$INSTALLER\win32\bin\win32 where $INSTALLER represents either the DVD folder or the folder where you downloaded the installer and .zip files.?Run the vcredist_x86.exe file.Once vcredist_x86.exe finishes installing the libraries, try running the installer again.

Summary: Resize block produces incorrect result for boolean input
Description: For boolean inputs, Resize block may produce incorrect results during simulation and it errors out during code generation.
Workaround: Follow these steps to process boolean inputs:1. Open your Simulink? model in MATLAB?.2. Place the Simulink/Signal Attributes Data Type Conversion block in front of the Resize block.3. Set the Data Type Conversion block output data type parameter to 'unit8'.4. Set the Resize block Interpolation Method parameter to 'Nearest Neighbor'.5. Place the Simulink/Logic and Bit Operations Compare to Zero block after the Resize block.6. Set the Compare to Zero block Operator parameter to '>' and the Output data type mode parameter to 'boolean'.7. Save your model.

Summary: Reverse Annotation in an XML Comparison Report does not work on Mac OS X 10.5
Description: Reverse Annotation in a Simulink Report Generator XML comparison report does not work on Mac OS X 10.5. When you click the hyperlinks in the report, nothing happens and the corresponding items in the original models are not displayed.

Summary: The FFT block of powerlib_extras does not work with signals coming out of Voltage Measurement or Current Measurement blocks
Description: If you connect a Voltage Measurement or Current Measurement block directly to an FFT block of powerlib_extras  and run the simulation, you get the following error: Error reported by S-function 'discreteNLastSamples' in 'mymodel/FFT/Discrete Shift Register/S-Function ': Invalid input or output port data type. An error occurred while propagating data type 'double' from 'broken/FFT/Discrete Shift Register/Out', input port 1.
Workaround: Connect a Signal Specification block at the input of the FFT block to ensure that the input type is double.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop software might generate uncompilable code for Stateflow chart
Description: If a model contains a Stateflow chart, Real-Time Workshop software might generate illegal C identifiers that start with a numeric, for example: boolean 0_sf_aVarTruthTableCondition;  In this case, the generated code does not compile.
Workaround: If you have Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software, on the Real-Time Workshop > Symbols pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, change the Identifier format control parameter, Local temporary variables, to generate a legal C identifier. For example, use the setting, tmp$N$M.

Summary: Passing character string for coord argument to H5S.select_elements causes segmentation fault
Description: The signature for the HDF5 low-level dataspace function H5S.select_elements  is:  H5S.select_elements(space_id, op, coord) where coord  is a 2-dimensional array of 0-based values specifying the coordinates of the elements being selected. If you specify a character value for this argument, such as 'H5S_ALL' , H5S.select_elements  will segfault. ? ?
Workaround: To avoid the possibility of a segfault, replace the low-level HDF5 mex-file using these instructions.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file     $MATLABROOT/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/@H5ML/HDF5lib.$MEXEXTwhere $MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function, and where $MEXEXT represents the filename extension for your platform, as returned by the mexext function. For example, on a 64-bit linux machine, copy HDF5lib.mexa64 to HDF5lib.mexa64.oldDownload and unzip the attached zip archive file. Copy the file that is specific to your platform (also identified by mexext) into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the     rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: 'filled' option in vec2mtx fills output with 0s
Description: When you apply function vec2mtx  to one or more polygons and use the 'filled'  option, the function should place the value 1 in each output grid cell intersected by a polygon edge, fill the polygon interiors with 0s, and fill the area outside the polygon(s) with 2s. Instead, the value 0 is assigned to the entire output grid.
Workaround: Please see  Bug Report 519161.

Summary: Subsystem code generation might fail when block sample time is specified through model or mask workspace
Description: If you specify the sample time for a block in a subsystem using a variable in the model or mask workspace, the code generation process might produce an error when: Generating C code from a subsystem Generating a S-function from a subsystem Generating exported functions from a subsystem  The error message is similar to the following:Error generating S-function for 'test01/Subsystem' in block diagram 'test01'. MATLAB error message: Cannot change the active configuration set during model compilation or simulation.

Summary: MATLAB Builder JA fails to build component on Windows when the path to a compiled file contains a directory beginning with lowercase 'u'
Description: This problem occurs because the sequence '\u ' is not properly escaped in all circumstances during the code generation phase of deployment.
Workaround: Rename the offending directory or move the file to a different directory. This allows the Java compiler to successfully compile the generated code.Use the command line version of mcc to compile the project. When building from deploytool, the full, expanded command line is displayed in the output window, as follows:Build output( 2008-11-18 11:14:46). mcc -F 'c:\work\u\DirectoryBeginningWithU.prj' Compiler version: 4.9 (R2008b) mcc -W 'java:directoryBeginningWithU,DirectoryBeginningWithU' -d 'c:\work\u\DirectoryBeginningWithU\src' -T 'link:lib' -v 'class{DirectoryBeginningWithU:c:\work\u\foo.m}' The command line can be modified to compile all of the files of interest in a way that will allow javac to process them after generation. Modifying the 'class' parameter passed to mcc like: mcc -W 'java:directoryBeginningWithU,DirectoryBeginningWithU' -d 'c:\work\u\DirectoryBeginningWithU\src' -T 'link:lib' -v 'class{DirectoryBeginningWithU:foo.m}' Will produce code that can be processed by javac.

Summary: Shared variables are full-range even in the main function
Description: In multitasking verifications, the multitasking part starts at the end of the main, and global variables should not be shared before multitasking begins. However, shared variables appear to be full range in the main itself.

Summary: Incorrect state order error when linearizing models built on the SimScape? Platform
Description: Using the Simulink? Control Design? linearization GUI to linearize a Simulink? model that was built on the SimScape? platform may result in the following error: The state order specified contains states that are no longer in the Simulink model. Remove these states from the Linearization State Ordering tab in the Options Dialog. The Options Dialog can be launched from the Tools Menu.  ?
Workaround: Replace the file [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\linearize.m as follows:Rename the file [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\linearize.m to linearize.bak.Download the attached file named linearize.m and save it in this directory.Restart MATLAB?.

Summary: The Kasami Sequence Generator block does not support shift parameter values greater than 255.
Description: The Kasami Sequence Generator block does not support shift parameter values greater than 255 due to the limiting value of an internal variable used in the implementation. This affects Kasami sequences whose generator polynomial has an order greater than 8.

Summary: Comments may not be generated for Product or Divide blocks
Description: If you select the Simulink block / Stateflow object comments  parameter in the Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane, the generated code might not include automatically generated comments for Product or Divide blocks when the Multiplication  parameter of either block is set to Matrix(*) . ?

Summary: Units are corrupted when exporting data to Excel from the Data Editor
Description: The units are corrupted when you export data to Excel from the Data Editor of the Model-Based Calibration toolbox software.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the file in the Attachments section to /toolbox/mbc/mbctools.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB block might cause a CGIR assertion error message during the build process
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail when all of the following conditions apply: The model has an Embedded MATLAB block. An input of the Embedded MATLAB block is discontiguous, for example, a mux signal from multiple sources.  The build process reports error messages similar to the following: CGIR assertion in .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_vcat_scal.cpp(1363): 'startEl <= regStart'   [0] libmwsimulink.dll:0x6f4c427e() + 1867268734 bytes   [1] cg_ir.dll:0x6d8636cb() + 1837512395 bytes   [2] rtwcg.dll:0x6ee4d780() + 1860491136 bytes   [3] rtwcg.dll:0x6ee4d979() + 1860491641 bytes   [4] rtwcg.dll:0x6ee4dd36() + 1860492598 bytes   [5] rtwcg.dll:0x6ee4df75() + 1860493173 bytes

Summary: Why am I unable to create a deployed application that uses the Image Acquisition ToolboxTM?
Description: When I try to create a deployed application using the Image Acquisition ToolboxTM  with the MATLAB? CompilerTM , it works fine on the development machine which has MATLAB installed, but I am unable to run it on a machine that only has the MCR. When I try running the compiled application I receive the following error: ??? Error using ==> imaqhwinfo at 61Invalid MEX-file 'C:\Temp\mcrCache7.9\testim1\toolbox\imaq\imaq\imaqmex.mexw32': The specified module could not be found.
Workaround: The problem occurs because the MATLAB Compiler is not including a file needed by the Image Acquisition Toolbox. It is possible to manually specify the needed file when compiling your application using the -a flag. For example:mcc -m myFile.m -a <matlabroot>/bin/win32/tamimframemex.dllIn the above command, replace <matlabroot> with the location where MATLAB is installed. This can be determined using the matlabroot command within MATLAB.It is also possible to update an Image Acquisition Toolbox file to fix this problem. To apply the patch, extract the file in the attached zip file to <matlabroot>\toolbox\imaq\mcc.enc replacing <matlabroot> with the location where MATLAB is installed.

Summary: "Too many input arguments" error when modifying axis limits in Property Editor
Description: Changing axis limits using Property Editor of Plot Tools can introduce commas in their values if they are greater than 1000 (thousands separators are added). This produces an error whenever these limits are changed in the Property Editor unless you edit out the commas. For example, x=1000:5000; plot(x,sin(x))  Open Plot Tools, select the X -axis, and change the first value of X Limits   from "1,000" to "1,500". An error dialog opens when you click away: ??? Error using ==> disp  Too many input arguments
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow these steps:1. Start MATLAB2. At the command prompt type: matlabroot. This displays the path to MATLAB root. Write down the path.3. Exit MATLAB4. Navigate to the matlab root directory and then to java directory within it.5. If there is no directory called patch under the java directory, create one there.6. Navigate to the directory called patch7. Create the following directory under the patch directory : com\mathworks\page\plottool\propertyeditor\controls (on Windows) or com/mathworks/page/plottool/propertyeditor/controls (on Unix and Mac)8. Copy the following three files (attached) to the newly created controls directory: LimitsControl$1.class LimitsControl$TextControlTransferHandler.class LimitsControl.class9. Restart MATLAB.You can also manually remove the commas from the Property Editor X Limits or Y Limits fields to prevent the error, but the commas will return if you edit the limits again.

Summary: Incorrect display of MFILT.IIRINTERP objects
Description: In certain cases the disp method of MFILT.IIRINTERP objects shows sections that do not exist. For example, >> d = fdesign.interpolator(2,'Halfband','TW,Ast',500,80,16e3);>> h = design(d,'ellip','FilterStructure','iirinterp')h =        FilterStructure: 'IIR Polyphase Interpolator'                                                                             Polyphase: Phase1: Section1: 0.0589752933014027                                 Section2: 0.40126875985101                                   Section3: 0.733401726405811                                  Section4: 0.952128660724001                          Phase2: Section1: 0.210896097258882                                 Section2: 0.582815252525032                                 Section3: 0.852596725205787                                 Section4: 0.952128660724001                                                       InterpolationFactor: 2                                                                                                 PersistentMemory: false  The display shows 4 sections for the phase2 filter, but it only has 3 sections: >> h.Polyphase.Phase2ans =     Section1: 0.2109    Section2: 0.5828    Section3: 0.8526 Attempting to access Section4 of Phase2 using h.Polyphase.Phase2.Section4 will therefore result in an error.

Summary: Breakpoints: MATLAB? software crashes when you set more than 128 breakpoints.
Description: MATLAB? software crashes when you set a large number of breakpoints in a file with multiple subfunctions. Specifically, the problem occurs when you set more than 128 breakpoints in the main function and more than 256 in the next one.
Workaround: There are number of possible workarounds. The simplest is to limit number of breakpoints in each function to less than 128. If it is necessary to set a breakpoint on each line in the file, then split functions into several subfunction with less than 128 executable lines in each.

Summary: Using Find and Replace in Embedded MATLAB Editor displays exception in command window
Description: Using Find and Replace  in the Embedded MATLAB Editor may throw an exception in the MATLAB command window. The functionality works correctly.

Summary: An object of a user-defined class with static properties is created. In some circumstances, when this object is cleared using CLEAR CLASSES, MATLAB hangs. The status shows "Busy".
Description: An object of a user-defined class with static properties is created. In some circumstances, when this object is cleared using CLEAR CLASSES, MATLAB hangs. The status shows "Busy".
Workaround: Run "clear all" before clear classes.clear all, clear classes

Summary: Segmentation violation in function bufferm given duplicate/nearly-coincident vertices
Description: For an input polygon with a sequence of duplicate or nearly-coincident vertices, you may encounter a segmentation violation in function bufferm  (originating in gpcmex).
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the bufferm.m function file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/bufferm.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: bufferm.m.oldCopy the version of bufferm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: solve can error on systems of equations involving exponents
Description: The command s = solve( ...   'Y = A^alpha * K^(1-alpha)',...   '(1-alpha)*Y = K*(1/bet-1 + delta)',...   'K = K*(1-delta) + T',...   'Y = C + T',...   'Y','C','T','K' ...); errors with the error message ??? Error using ==> subsrefIndex exceeds matrix dimensions.Error in ==> sym.subsref at 16  y = builtin('subsref',struct(x),a);Error in ==> solve at 191      S.(char(symvars(j))) = R(:,j);

Summary: MATLAB Compiler User Guide contains incorrect UNIX paths
Description: In the MATLAB Compiler User's Guide, in "Directories Required for Development and Testing" > "UNIX Paths for Run-Time Deployment" the pathnames contain the directory name jre1.6.0.  This is not correct. The correct directory name is simply jre.  For example, the path matlabroot/sys/java/jre/glnx86/jre1.6.0/lib/i386/native_threads  should read: matlabroot/sys/java/jre/glnx86/jre/lib/i386/native_threads  ? ? ?
Workaround: There is no workaround except to use the correct directory name of jre.

Summary: The demo model, c2808pmsmsim, generates an error when you try to build the model using Embedded IDE Link CC.
Description: The demo model, c2808pmsmsim, generates the following error message when you try to build the model using Embedded IDE Link CC: %% BEGIN ERROR "Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'c2808pmsmsim'. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> tlc_c at 116 Block 'c2808pmsmsim/External torque simulation signal/FromWs' uses continuous time, which is not supported with the current configuration. Consider selecting the support 'continuous time' option on the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Interface page" %% END ERROR The main purpose of this demo is plant-controller simulation. The demo is correctly configured - the block in question is to be used for simulation and it does not support discrete-time solver.

Summary: NCFSYN discrete-time case errors
Description: [K,CL,GAM,INFO]=ncfsyn(G,W1,W2) errored out for discrete-time G when optional weights W1 and W2 were present.

Summary: error messages when trying to display a large symbolic expression
Description: I have a symbolic expression y  with length > 1 million characters. When I try to display it at the command prompt by executing the variable name, I get the following error message: >> y??? Error using ==>mupadengine.mupadengine>mupadengine.evalinat 121Error: Internal error. Please visit the web page https://www.mathworks.com/support/contact_us.html for technical support

Summary: Microsoft Visual C++ 2008 (Microsoft Visual Studio 9.0) is not supported by Embedded MATLAB Function blocks, Stateflow charts, or Truth Table blocks in R2008b.
Description: The Microsoft Visual C++ 2008 (Microsoft Visual Studio 9.0) is not supported by the following blocks in R2008b: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks, Stateflow Charts, and Truth Table blocks. If you choose this compiler as the MEX compiler using mex -setup  on 64-bit Windows platforms, you will get the following build error: The mex compiler 'Microsoft Visual C++ 2008' is not supported for simulation builds.Use 'mex -setup' to select a supported compiler. If you select this compiler on 32-bit Windows platforms using mex -setup , the LCC compiler is substituted and you do not receive a build error.
Workaround: To build your model with the Microsoft Visual C++ 2008 (Microsoft Visual Studio 9.0) compiler on 32-bit and 64-bit Windows platforms, apply the following patch:Back up the following four files:matlab\toolbox\stateflow\coder\private\code_msvc_make_file.mmatlab\toolbox\stateflow\coder\private\compute_compiler_info.mmatlab\toolbox\stateflow\coder\private\create_file_name_info.mmatlab\toolbox\stateflow\stateflow\private\compilerman.mSubstitute the attachments.Execute the command rehash toolboxreset.Restart MATLAB.Run mex -setup and select Microsoft Visual C++ 2008 (Microsoft Visual Studio 9.0) as your compiler.Simulate your model.

Summary: MATLAB Builder NE WebFigures fails with certain locale settings.
Description: On servers that have locales set to a use commas in place of decimal points for double values (such as German or French), some WebFigures fail to render.
Workaround: Set the site's locale to English. To do this, add the attached XML to the Web.config file.

Summary: Memory leak in CONTOURF with Zbuffer renderer
Description: The leak is easier to verify for larger matrices. Execute the following: x=100;y=100;z=rand(100);ax=axes;for i = 1:10 [C,h]=contourf(x,y,z,20);end    Memory usage grows during each loop iteration.
Workaround: The memory leak occurs only for the Zbuffer renderer, which is the default. The workaround is to use the OpenGL renderer instead. Insert the following after the call to axes:set(ax,'renderer','opengl');

Summary: Structures defined and referenced in #include header files may not be properly defined in MATLAB.
Description: If the following code exists in one or more header files included by the main header file, then MATLAB does not correctly represent the structure A inside of hasEmbeddedStructs . struct A { int p1; short p2;};  struct hasEmbeddedStructs { double pd; struct A a; };  ?
Workaround: Use the loadlibrary 'addheader' option to add the needed header files.?

Summary: Import wizard fails to open Microsoft Excel .xlsx files with more than one sheet
Description: The Import Wizard is not able to import .xlsx  (Microsoft Office 2007) files containing more than one sheet. When you attempt to do so, the following error displays: "Error using --> open at 157 First input argument, VARNAME, must be either a string or a cell array of strings."
Workaround: If you are running MATLAB 2008b, work around this issue either byRe-saving the spreadsheet in .xls format (the old Excel format) and importing that fileInstall the attached patch to work with .xlsx, .xlsb and .xlsm files by downloading the attached zip archive, office07PatchForR2008b.zip, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following files. ????(a)?matlabroot\matlab\java\jar\jmi.jar????(b)??matlabroot\matlab\java\jar\mlwidgets.jar????(c)??matlabroot\matlab\toolbox\matlab\codetools\uiimport.m????(d)??matlabroot\matlab\toolbox\matlab\iofun\importdata.m????(e)??matlabroot\matlab\toolbox\matlab\iofun\xlsfinfo.m????(f)??matlabroot\matlab\toolbox\matlab\iofun\xlsread.m????(g)??matlabroot\matlab\toolbox\matlab\general\finfo.m?matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file by adding "old" to the file extension: jmi.jar.oldDownload the attached zip archive, office07PatchForR2008b.zip, storing it in the ?matlabroot directory.Extract files from the archive, using the folder names stored in the zip file.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.Note that the patch is not guaranteed to work on releases of MATLAB earlier than R2008b (version 7.7).

Summary: Incorrect linearization results for referenced model in normal mode containing a non-virtual subsystem with direct connection of subsystem inputs and outputs
Description: You may get incorrect linearization results for a Simulink? model when all of the following are true: Model contains at least one normal-mode model reference Referenced model contains a non-virtual subsystem Non-virtual subsystem has subsystem inputs connected directly to subsystem outputs  See the attached model for an example of this construct. The subsystem "refmdl/Atomic Subsystem" has these characteristics.
Workaround: In the non-virtual subsystem, add a signal conversion block between the subsystem input and subsystem output to eliminate the direct connection.Turn off the Block Reduction option in one of the following places:At the command line by updating the linoptions object.In the Linearization Task Options dialog box. To open this dialog box, select Tools > Options in the Control and Estimation Tools Manager.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops when using UNC paths with the -include option
Description: When you run a PolySpace verification with the "-include" option to include a header file that has an UNC path such as:-include "\\dir\poly\header.h"  The verification stops during the compilation phase with the following message:catastrophic error: could not open source file "/dir/poly/header.h"
Workaround: To workaround this issue, map a local drive to the directory on the UNC path.For example, on a Windows system, double-click the My Computer icon, then select Tools > Map Network Drive.If you map a UNC path to a local drive letter (such as M), you will be able to use the'-include' option with the following syntax:-include "M:\dir\poly\header.h"

Summary: When both the puncture pattern and the erasure pattern are specified as empty vectors in the 'vitdec' function, or when only the erasure pattern is specified as anempty vector, a segmentation violation error occurs.
Description: A segmentation violation error occurs in Matlab when the last optional argument is set to empty in the 'vitdec' function. This happens with both hard and soft decission options. Examples where this problem will be seen: PUNCPAT = ones(1,24); PUNCPAT([ 2 4 10 20]) = 0; vitdec(zeros(1,20), t, 8, 'cont', 'hard',PUNCPAT,[]) or vitdec(zeros(1,20), t, 8, 'cont', 'hard',[],[])
Workaround: PUNCPAT and ERASPAT cannot be both empty arrays. If you would like to use the default values, do not specify these arguments. If you want to specify only ERASPAT, then specify PUNCPAT as an empty array.If you want to specify a non-default PUNCPAT and use the default ERASPAT value, then specify PUNCPAT only and do not set the last argument (corresponding to ERASPAT) to empty.

Summary: Concatenate block may produce incorrect result if one of its nonvirtual source inputs has the same signal dimensions as its output signal
Description: The Concatenate block may attempt to perform an inplace computation and produce an incorrect result when ALL of the following conditions are true: The Concatenate block has two or more inputs. A virtual block, such as the Selector or the Mux block, drives one of the inputs to the Concatenate block (see Simulink documentation on virtual blocks). The first nonvirtual source block driving this virtual block has an output signal with the same dimensions as the output of the Concatenate block.  AND at least ONE of the following conditions is true: The parameter Mode  is set to Multidimensional array  and the parameter Concatenate dimension  is less than the number of dimensions of one or more of the input signals. For example, the Concatenate dimension is two and one of the input signals is three-dimensional. The Concatenate block has both real and complex input signals.
Workaround: To work around this issue, apply one of the following methods:Turn off the Signal storage reuse option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.For each input port of the Concatenate block that is driven by a virtual block, insert a Signal Conversion block between the virtual block and the Concatenate block . Also, select the Override optimizations and always copy signal option of the Signal Conversion Function Block Parameters pane.

Summary: Saved minimum order filter designs created in the command line or in fdatool in MATLAB versions R2007b and older will not load correctly in MATLAB versions R2008a and R2008b.
Description: In MATLAB versions R2008a and R2008b, if you load a minimum order filter design object or fdatool session that was previously saved in MATLAB R2007b or older, and if the MatchExactly property is set to a non-default value, MATLAB generates errors. This applies to low pass, high pass, band pass, and band stop minimum order filters designed with Butterworth, Chebyshev I, Chebyshev II, or elliptical algorithms. Errors will be seen instantly when loading a saved fdatool session. No error will be seen when loading a filter design object on the command line. However, errors will be seen when applying a method to the loaded design object H such as fvtool(H), drawmask(H), or info(H,'long').

Summary: At the end of the example on the copulafit reference page, the code is incorrect and draws the wrong figure.
Description: The example on the copulafit  reference page is intended to illustrate how to generate new random data having the same marginal distributions and the same correlation as the original data. However, the marginal distributions of the new data are not correct. As a result, the axes of the final plot do not cover the same range as those of the original plot.
Workaround: In the following two lines u and v should be replaced with x and y, respectively:x1 = ksdensity(u,u1,'function','icdf');y1 = ksdensity(v,v1,'function','icdf');These lines should read:x1 = ksdensity(x,u1,'function','icdf');y1 = ksdensity(y,v1,'function','icdf');

Summary: Display block might not update display when decimation is greater than one
Description: The Display block does not update its display at the initial time if decimation is greater than one. This unexpected behavior results in cases where the display is not updated at all. For instance, under the following conditions, Display block will not update its display: A Constant block with a sample time set to 'inf' is connected to a Display block. Decimation of the Display block is greater than one. The Inline parameters  check box is selected in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Simulink.Timeseries modifies user specified time data
Description: When time field in Simulink.Timeseries is set to something like 0.001*[0:1:1000], the queried time (ts.Time) differs from the original time used to set the time field.

Summary: Copying an implicit link from a library to a model results in a bad link
Description: If a library has a linked block to a different library, and one of the child subsystems of the linked block is copied to a new model, the result is a bad link. The link points to the library that contains the linked block, rather than the library that contains the original library block
Workaround: Copy the required subsystem from the original library.

Summary: CONVENC function throws an error when a puncture pattern is specified and [] is specified as the default initial state
Description: The help documentation on the CONVENC function mentions that one could specify 0 or [] to use the default value for init_state. However, when executing the following commands on the MATLAB command prompt: t = poly2trellis([3 3],[4 5 7;7 4 2]); convenc([zeros(1, 4)], t, [0 1 0], []); The following error message is observed: ??? Error using ==> convenc at 151 The initial state must be an integer scalar between 0 and (TRELLIS.numStates-1). See POLY2TRELLIS.
Workaround: If initial state is not defined, i.e., the following syntax is usedt = poly2trellis([3 3],[4 5 7;7 4 2]);convenc([zeros(1, 4)], t, [0 1 0]);then the default initial value, which is 0, is used.

Summary: National Instruments PCI-6601 or PCI-6602 Armed Pulse Generation block might not detect an input change if the block is disarmed
Description: If your model contains the National Instruments PCI-6601 or PCI-6602 Armed Pulse Generation block, and you change the input values to the block while the block is disarmed, the model output might not be correct when the block is next armed. This issue occurs only with input values that infrequently change. If you change the values again with the block armed, the model output will be correct.
Workaround: If you have xPC Target version 2.9 (R2006a), as a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x.c to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x_bak.cRename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x.mexw32 to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x_bak.mexw32Unzip the file ctrarm2006a.zip.Copy ctrarmnipci660x.c and ctrarmnipci660x.mexw32 to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.Restart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.If you have xPC Target version other than 2.9 (R2006a), as a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x.c to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x_bak.cRename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x.mexw32 to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\ctrarmnipci660x_bak.mexw32Unzip the file ctrarm2006a.zip.Copy ctrarmnipci660x.c and ctrarmnipci660x.mexw32 to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocksChange directory to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks.Mex the ctrarmnipci660x.c file with the command: mex ctrarmnipci660x.cRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: Unable to change font face selection in the Insert Text block.
Description: The Font face  selection list is missing from the Font tab. It appears only as an edit box instead of a popup menu with multiple fonts.
Workaround: Follow these steps to install files to use with R2008b.1. Quit MATLAB?.2. Unzip the attached ZIP-file to the following location: MATLABROOT/toolbox/vipblks/vipblks/@vipdialog, where MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine.3. Restart MATLAB.4. Issue the following command:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Digital I/O pins on MCC USB-ERB24 do not correspond to actual pins on the device
Description: When you use the Measurement Computing USB-ERB24, the applied signal does not activate the expected pin on the device. For example, applying a voltage to pin 0 activates pin 17 on the device. Incorrect information in the MCC initialization file causes this behavior.
Workaround: To work around this issue:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Find the MCC initialization file, mwmcc.ini:$MATLABROOT\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwmcc.ini(where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root installation directory that you can obtain by issuing:matlabroot at the MATLAB Command Window.)3. Make a copy of this file and rename it to mwmcc.ini.old.4. Open the file mwmcc.ini in a text editor, and search for the section for the USB-ERB24. Delete the existing section and replace it with the following:[USB-ERB24]ID = 0x8aBus = USBDIOConfigurable = 1DPORTS = 4DPORT0LINES = 8DPORT0TYPE = 10DPORT0CONFIG = 2DPORT1LINES = 8DPORT1TYPE = 11DPORT1CONFIG = 2DPORT2LINES = 4DPORT2TYPE = 12DPORT2CONFIG = 2DPORT3LINES = 4DPORT3TYPE = 13DPORT3CONFIG = 25. Save the file mwmcc.ini and restart MATLAB.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops at the beginning of compilation phase with the message: 'Error: Empty argument not allowed.'
Description: Launching PolySpace verification on C code using the Launcher GUI, the verification stops with the following message in the 'Compile log' window: Error: Empty argument not allowed.  This problem happens when the option -dialect iar  or -dialect keil  is selected in conjunction with the option "Send to PolySpace Server " without specifying any value for the option -sfr-types .
Workaround: Restart the verification with the option -sfr-types "sfr=8".?

Summary: The MuPAD commands bool(PI<PI) and bool(PI>PI) error.
Description: The MuPAD commands bool(PI<PI)  and bool(PI>PI)  issue the following error message instead of returning FALSE: Error: Can't evaluate to boolean [_less]
Workaround: Convert the values to floating point values bool(float(PI<PI)) and bool(float(PI>PI)).

Summary: The MuPAD command testtype gives an error when checking whether a list is Odd or Even
Description: The following code in MuPAD returns the expected output of FALSE , FALSE  datlist:=[1,2,3,4];testtype(datlist,Type::Prime),testtype(datlist,Type::Complex)  However the following command issues the error message instead of returning FALSE: testtype(datlist,Type::Even)  Error: Illegal operand [divide];during evaluation of 'Type::Even'

Summary: Error generating ASAP2 file for state that refers to signal object with Auto or SimulinkGlobal storage class
Description: If you try to generate an ASAP2 file for a model that contains a state that refers to a signal object for which the storage class is set to Auto  or SimulinkGlobal , the TLC code for generating the ASAP2 file errors out with a message similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\asap2lib.tlc Line: 491 Column: 31Undefined identifier DataTypeIdx
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform either of the following actions:Update the signal object to use a storage class other than Auto or SimulinkGlobal. Replace the shipping asap2lib.tlc with the attached asap2lib.tlc, using the following steps:Rename the file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/asap2lib.tlc, where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root. For example, add .old to the end of the file name.Download the attached .zip file using the link below and extract asap2lib.tlc to the same location as the file you renamed.Restart your MATLAB session and retry the build.

Summary: The Bernoulli Binary Generator block is slower in R2008B compared to previous releases (R2008A).
Description: In R2008B, the Bernoulli Binary Generator block was updated to use the Simulink Constant block. In making the change, its mask helper function was updated to use the mat2str function which was unnecessary.
Workaround: Replace the file (commblkbernoullisrc2.m) included in the attached .zip file, with the one in the MATLABROOT/toolbox/commblks/commmasks/ folder.This should improve the performance of the block.

Summary: On the Macintosh, windows created by compiled applications cannot obtain input focus
Description: On the Macintosh, windows created by compiled applications cannot obtain input focus. Although, the windows respond to mouse clicks on their menu bar as well as maximize, minimize and termination requests, input cannot be entered into text fields.
Workaround: If the application is executed as part of an application bundle, the application correctly responds to input focus requests.For release R2008b, to achieve this workaround, download the attached prototype application bundle. After downloading, unzip the attached file named myapp.app. Unzipping the file creates a hierarchy of directories and files. To use the bundle with your application:Rename myapp.app to yourappname.app, where yourappname is the name of your application.Edit myapp.app/Contents/Info.plist. Wherever myapp appears replace with yourappname.Replace myapp.app/Contents/MacOS/myapp with your executable (yourappname).

Summary: The command "clear classes" fails in certain circumstances.
Description: Consider the case where you have a class constructor that passes an anonymous function to an inputParser object method, as shown below: classdef wafer_info      methods        function obj = wafer_info(varargin)            strchk = @(x)ischar(x) && size(x,1);            p = inputParser;            p.addParamValue('mask' ,[],strchk);            p.addParamValue('die' ,[],strchk);      end          end    end  If you execute the command "clear classes" on this class, MATLAB may throw the following warning: obj = wafer_info; clear classes;Warning: Objects of <> class exist.  Cannot clear this class or any of its super-classes. If you get this warning, you must restart MATLAB to clear the class.
Workaround: You can clear the inputParser before the class goes out of scope.

Summary: textscan %q throws an error when reading an empty quoted string.
Description: The textscan  function handles an empty quoted string improperly when used with the %q conversion specifier. The following code returns an error indicating that MATLAB is unable to read the string:   HeaderLine = '"a","b","c","d","e","070","f","g","h","u","v",""';   TempData = textscan (HeaderLine , '%q' , 'Delimiter' , ',');
Workaround: There are no work-arounds to this bug at this time.

Summary: Dialog for mpt data objects does not show Enum: <class name> on the data type pulldown
Description: The dialog for mpt.Signal  and mpt.Parameter  objects does not include Enum:  <class name>  as an option for the DataType property.
Workaround: You can set the data type property to be an enumerated data type by manually entering the data type as Enum: ClassName (substituting the name of your enumerated class) or by using the Data Type Assistant.

Summary: Running mcc with a read-only directory causes an error.
Description: mcc  allows you to pass in a destination directory as an option input parameters. When it is not passed in, the current directory is used as a destination directory. If mcc  is run without passing a destination directory, and your current directory is read-only, mcc  fails and produces an unclear error message:   mccExcludedFiles.log: Permission denied ??? Error using ==> mcc Error executing mcc, return status = -1073741811.
Workaround: Ensure the directory you are executing mcc is writable, or provide a destination directory using the -d flag that is writable.

Summary: Removing signals in a Bus Selector may cause incorrect updates in downstream Bus Selectors
Description: When a Bus Selector block provides the input for one or more downstream Bus Selector blocks, the downstream block's selections may be erroneously updated. The problem occurs if both of the following are true: The selected item removed from the upstream Bus Selector is not the last item in the list The downstream Bus Selector selects the same signal element that was removed from the upstream Bus Selector  In this situation, the signal that was removed by the upstream Bus Selector and is selected in the downstream Bus Selector may be replaced by another, arbitrary selection, rather than being updated to show that it is not a valid selection anymore. The model may continue to update and simulate without generating an error, but will probably give incorrect results.

Summary: TMTOOL generates non-executable code for interface objects
Description: If you use TMTool to create an interface object, you will see that the generated code has lines with incorrect syntax. This occurs when you get the code in the session log for a session using the interface objects only: % Find a GPIB object.obj1 = instrfind('Type', 'gpib', 'BoardIndex', 7, 'PrimaryAddress', 18, 'Tag', '');% Create the GPIB object if it does not exist% otherwise use the object that you find if isempty(obj1)obj1 = gpib('AGILENT', 7, 18);elsefclose(obj1);obj1 = obj1(1)end% Configure instrument object, obj1set(obj1, InputBufferSize, 1000000); %This line will not work% Configure instrument object, obj1set(obj1, OutputBufferSize, 512); %This line will not work% Configure instrument object, obj1.set(obj1, 'ByteOrder', 'bigEndian');set(obj1, 'InputBufferSize', 100000);
Workaround: Download the attached file, mcodewriter.zip, to c:\temp\mcodewriter.zip.Start MATLAB and execute the following commands:>>cd(matlabroot) % this makes sure you are in the right directory>>unzip('c:\temp\mcodewriter.zip'); % this will patch MATLAB with the file listed below.To remove the fix, perform the following.>>cd(matlabroot);>> !del patch\com\mathworks\toolbox\instrument\instrcomm\MCodeWriter.class

Summary: Compilation error when using TIC6000 Block Processing block
Description: When building a model containing a TIC6000 Block Processing block, project build fails with the following error reported in CCS: "csl_dat3.c", line 7: fatal error: could not open source file "csl_dat3.h"1 fatal error detected in the compilation of "csl_dat3.c".Compilation terminated.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_497960.zip. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_497960.zip', 'install')Follow instructions printed out on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation. The attached patch modifies the following files in your MATLAB installation:<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000blks\include\bp\csl_dat3.h?

Summary: Designing compensators for multi-rate models while using simulation snapshots to create operating points results in an error
Description: Designing compensators for multi-rate models in the Simulink? Compensator Design? Task results in the following error when you use simulation snapshots to create operating points. This error occurs when you click the Tune Blocks  button. Your Simulink Model cannot be analyzed due to the following error:Attempt to reference field of non-structure array
Workaround: In the Control and Estimation Tools Manager, select Tools > Options. In the Options dialog box, set one of the following options:Rate Conversion Method to 'tustin' or 'prewarp'Design Sample Time to a value greater than zero

Summary: signalbuilder function set command changes the values of the wrong signal(s) or group(s)
Description: Using the signalbuilder  function set  command to set or modify signal(s) or group(s) values might update the wrong signal(s) or group(s). This incorrect behavior can occur if the signal(s) or group(s) you are modifying are not active when you perform the update.
Workaround: If you have R2008a, to work around this issue:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Rename matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/cmdApi.p tomatlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/cmdApi.p_old.3. Download cmdApi_r2008ad.p and rename it to cmdApi.p.4. Save this file to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private.5. Restart MATLABIf you have R2008b, to work around this issue:1. Quit MATLAB.2. Rename matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/cmdApi.p tomatlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/cmdApi.p_old.3. Download cmdApi_r2008bd.p and rename it to cmdApi.p.4. Save this file to matlabroot/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private.5. Restart MATLAB

Summary: The mech_import or import_physmod command fails to successfully import XML file with many frame elements
Description: If a SimMechanics XML file exported from a CAD system has a large number of frame elements (larger than 200) then the mech_import  or import_physmod   command fails, and the following error message appears: ??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.Error in ==> S:\aphysmod\matlab\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@JointUpdater\getNodesToConnect.p>getNodesToConnect at 13edgeA.body = baseCS.getParent;Error in ==> S:\aphysmod\matlab\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@Graph\getNodesToConnect.p>getNodesToConnect at 11    nodesToConnect = horzcat(nodesToConnect,nodes{idx}.getNodesToConnect);Error in ==> S:\aphysmod\matlab\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@Updater\getNodesToConnect.p>getNodesToConnect at 6    nodesToConnect = thisUpdater.Graph.getNodesToConnect;...
Workaround: Rename the file $MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\mech\importer\+PMImport\@Updater\Updater.p to Updater_old.p. ($MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory as returned by the matlabroot command.)Download and place the attached Updater.p file in the above directory.Enter the following commands at the MATLAB command prompt:clear allclear classesrehash toolboxcache

Summary: The Bus Selector block dialog can issue a spurious error message
Description: If you select multiple signals in the Selected signals  pane of the Bus Selector block dialog box, then click Remove , an error message may appear even though no error occurred. The error message is similar to:    Error: busSelectorddg_cb Operands to the || and && operators   must be convertible to logical scalar values.
Workaround: The error message is harmless and can safely be ignored. The selected signals are removed.

Summary: All signals in SPTOOL are displayed with the same color.
Description: When you select multiple signals from the signal area of SPTOOL, the signal browser shows all the signals with the same color.
Workaround: Replace the $MATLABROOT$\toolbox\signal\sptoolgui\sigbrowse.m file with the attached file. Delete $MATLABROOT$\toolbox\signal\sptoolgui\sigbrowse.p file.

Summary: An assertion failure in the Stateflow Editor may occur when connecting two junctions that are close together.
Description: In Stateflow if you have two junctions close together and try to connect them with a constrained line (press and hold shift before starting to draw), an assertion stack trace may be generated.
Workaround: Do not use the constrained line option in Stateflow.

Summary: The MATLAB process locale setting is partially set to "C" locale on Macintosh OS X 10.5.x.
Description: On Mac OS X, MATLAB sets its process locale based on the value specified on the Formats tab on the International preference panel. In MATLAB version 7.6 (R2008a) and version 7.7 (R2008b), the process locale is partially set to "C" locale. A typical symptom is the month name format in a string date value returned by the DIR  function is always displayed in English. For example: a=dir;a(1).date  MATLAB always displays: ans =15-Aug-2008 12:41:18

Summary: Deployed applications cannot contain MATLAB object system class hierarchies with abstract methods.
Description: Definitions of MATLAB classes may contain empty functions. An empty function consists of a MATLAB file that contains only a function line and optional comments. Such functions are often called "abstract" methods, because they define an interface abstractly: they specify a function name and the number of inputs and outputs, but contain no executable code. Abstract methods serve as placeholders for subclasses to overload; they ensure that all subclasses implement the same interface. For example, the MATLAB class series  contains the abstract method combinePeerBrushingArrays , which consists of this M-code: function combinePeerBrushingArrays(h) % no op for parent class  Subclasses of series  such as areaseries  override combinePeerBrushingArrays , implementing it to perform subclass-specific actions. Deployed applications that make use of MATLAB classes with abstract methods will likely encounter error messages indicating that empty MATLAB files cannot be executed, such as: File is empty  ??? The MATLAB file "C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008a\toolbox\compiler\mcr\matlab\datamanager\@datamanager\@series\combinePeerBrushingArrays.m" cannot be executed.
Workaround: In certain cases, it may be possible to work around this bug by using mcc -a (additional files switch).Explicitly specifying the topmost class in the hierarchy as an additional file may cause mcc to properly include the abstract functions. For example, in the series case, specify the topmost class in the series hierarchy, datamanager, as an additional file via the -a switch, as follows:mcc -a ./datamanager ... If the topmost class is part of the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR), you must first copy the entire class hierarchy to your project directory before attempting to use it in a deployed application. Using the series example, you would need to copy $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/datamanager to your project directory before issuing the mcc command.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you run demo power_3level in Ideal Switching mode with all options turned on
Description: The demo entitled Three-Level PWM Converter and Dead Time (power_3level.mdl)  does not work in Ideal Switching mode. If you change the solver to ode23t set Simulation type  to Continuous , and select Enable use of ideal switching devices , Disable snubbers in switching devices , Disable On resistance in switching devices (Ron=0) , and Disable Forward voltage in switching devices (Vf=0)  in the Powergui block parameters dialog box, MATLAB will crash when you try to run this demo.

Summary: On Mac OS X, MATLAB sometimes misidentifies the user-specified UI language setting.
Description: On Macintosh OS X platforms, if a language is supported by more than one locale, MATLAB correctly identifies the language with only one of the locales. For all other locales, MATLAB displays the MATLAB:I18n:InconsistentUiLanguage  warning message, and incorrectly sets JVM locale and UI language. For example, if the language setting is "French" and the locale setting is "Canada (French)", MATLAB displays the following message: MATLAB:I18n:InconsistentUiLanguage - The user UI language setting, fr_FR.ISO8859-1, is different from the user locale setting, fr_CA.ISO8859-1.  MATLAB then sets JVM locale and UI Language to fr_FR.ISO8859-1 , instead of fr_CA.ISO8859-1 .

Summary: Unexpected MATLAB? or M-Lint termination: While editing a file with multiple syntax errors and M-Lint enabled, M-Lint or MATLAB suddenly terminates.
Description: Editing certain programs with multiple syntax errors and M-Lint enabled, can cause either M-Lint or MATLAB ? software to suddenly terminate. Because even the shortest known code example that causes this problem is quite complicated and has multiple syntax errors in it, The Mathworks? expects this behavior to occur rarely.
Workaround: See "About M-Lint and Unexpected MATLAB? Session Termination" in the MATLAB documentation for details on how to work around this problem.

Summary: Print log option fails when printing to PDF
Description: Selecting the Include Print Log  option on the Simulink Print  dialog box has no effect when printing a model to the PDF writer on a Microsoft Windows system.

Summary: Use of multi-assignment in setup section causes crash
Description: Statements such as: [x,y] = f  in the setup  section of a Simscape file cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Return multiple outputs as a structure.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when executing the methods function on a class inherited from a non-existent super-class.
Description: If a class is defined to be inherited from another class and the super-class is not on the MATLAB search path or is non-existent, executing the methods  function on the inherited class gives an segmentation violation. This can also happen if there are any errors detected during the parsing of the class.

Summary: The BT product parameter in the GMSK demodulator block was incorrectly not allowed to be greater than 1
Description: If BT product in GMSK demodulator is set to a value greater than 1, it will throw the following error: Gaussian BT product must be a real positive scalar.  This is incorrect behavior. Although the most often used BT product value is in the range 0.3 to 0.5, the value can be higher. GMSK with BT=inf is actually equivalent to MSK.
Workaround: Edit the following file:matlab/toolbox/commblks/commmasks/commblkcpmdemod.mand replace the existing checking for the BT parameter  if(~isscalar(maskBT) || ~isreal(maskBT) || (maskBT < 0) || (maskBT > 1))with if(~isscalar(maskBT) || ~isreal(maskBT) || (maskBT < 0))

Summary: Error using NI USB-9233 and NI USB-9234 when NI-DAQmx 8.8 driver is installed
Description: When you create an analog input object for either the USB-9233 or USB-9234: ai = analoginput('nidaq','Dev1')  You will see this error: ??? Error using ==> analoginput.analoginput at 99 Error using ==> analoginput.analoginput>localCreateAnalogInputObject at 193 NIDAQmx error : Measurements: Task contains physical channels on one or more devices that require you to specify the Sample Clock Rate. Specify a Sample Clock Rate. Task Name: MWDAT0002 Status Code: -201087   This occurs when you install the NI-DAQmx 8.8 driver and MATLAB R2008a or higher.
Workaround: Currently the only known workaround is to install the NI-DAQmx 8.7.1 driver.

Summary: The parameter D in the documentation for the constructor gbm is defined differently at two different places
Description: The 2008a documentation explains the parameter D  of the gbm  constructor as: 'an NVARS-by-NVARS diagonal matrix, where each element along the main diagonal is the square root of the corresponding element of the state vector.' The correct definition for the D  parameter of the gbm  constructor is: D  is an NVARS -by-NVARS  diagonal matrix, where each element along the main diagonal is the corresponding element of the state vector Xt  .

Summary: Segmentation violation when calling dragrect function with invalid arguments, such as a structure
Description: Function dragrect  causes a segment violation when it is invoked with a struct argument. The function is expects an n-by-4  matrix as the first argument and a numeric scalar as second argument. The segmentation violation occurs due to a failure to properly validate input arguments.

Summary: Rapid Accelerator always rebuilds if Real-Time Workshop is not installed
Description: The Rapid Accelerator simulation should rebuild only if structural changes are made to the model. However, because of a coding error, the Rapid Accelerator rebuilds before every simulation, regardless of any type of changes to the model. This problem is caused by an incorrect version check in the setup code for Rapid Accelerator simulation. This bug is only manifest if Real-Time Workshop is not installed; there is no license checkout issue.
Workaround: Complete the following steps for the R2008a release (Simulink 7.1):1. Delete the '$MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private/build_rapid_accel_target.p' file.2. Copy the attached 'build_rapid_accel_target.p.R2008a' file into the '$MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private' directory.3. Rename the copied file as 'build_rapid_accel_target.p'4. At the MATLAB command prompt, type 'clear all'.5. At the MATLAB command prompt, type 'rehash toolboxcache'.?Complete the following steps for the R2008b release (Simulink 7.2):1. Delete the '$MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private/build_rapid_accel_target.p' file.2. Copy the attached 'build_rapid_accel_target.p.R2008b' file into the '$MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private' directory.3. Rename the copied file as 'build_rapid_accel_target.p'4. At the MATLAB command prompt, type 'clear all'.5. At the MATLAB command prompt, type 'rehash toolboxcache'.?Complete the following steps for the R2008a+ release (Simulink 7.1):1. Delete the '$MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private/build_rapid_accel_target.p' file.2. Copy the attached 'build_rapid_accel_target.p.R2008a+' file into the '$MATLABROOT/toolbox/simulink/simulink/private' directory.3. Rename the copied file as 'build_rapid_accel_target.p'4. At the MATLAB command prompt, type 'clear all'.5. At the MATLAB command prompt, type 'rehash toolboxcache'.?

Summary: Deployed Java component that uses WebFigures fails to run when any toolboxes are deselected from the Toolbox on Path page in deploytool
Description: In deploytool , if any toolbox is deselected on the Toolbox on Path page in the Settings dialog, matlab/toolbox/javabuilder  is taken off depfun 's search path. This causes deployed Java applications that use WebFigures to fail, since matlab/toolbox/javabuilder/javawebfigure.m  is not included in the generated CTF archive.
Workaround: There are three workarounds for this issue:Select all the toolboxes in the Toolbox on Path page in the Settings dialog.
