This ensures matlab/toolbox/javabuilder will be included in depfun's search path.Add matlab/toolbox/javabuilder/javawebfigure.m in the Other files folder in your deploytool project.If you use mcc, add matlab/toolbox/javabuilder to depfun's search path using the -p flag.

Summary: Linearizing a model with linearization I/O points inside a normal mode model reference results in an error
Description: Linearizing a model may result in one of the following errors when a linearization I/O point is located on a block port inside a normal mode model reference that contains more than one output port. ??? In an assignment A(I) = B, the number of elements in B and  I must be the same.  Error in ==> LinearizationObjects.linearizationutil.postProcessJacobian at 62  J.Mi.InputPorts(ct) = ph.Outport;  Error in ==> LinearizationObjects.linearizationutil.getJacobian at 10J = postProcessJacobian(this,get_param(model,'Jacobian'));Error in ==> linearize>LocalLinearizeModel at 418  J_iter = getJacobian(linutil,model);  Error in ==> linearize at 212  [sys,userdef_stateName,J,iostruct] =  LocalLinearizeModel(ModelParameterMgr,io,op,opt,'io');
Workaround: Replace the file [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObject\@linearizationutil\postProcessJacobian.m as follows:Rename the file [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObject\@linearizationutil\postProcessJacobian.m to postProcessJacobian.bak.Download one of the following attached files to the same directory:postProcessJacobian_R2008a.m for the R2008a release of MATLAB?postProcessJacobian_R2008b.m for the R2008b release of MATLAB?Rename the file to postProcessJacobian.m.Restart MATLAB?.

Summary: Scalar min and max called with mixed integer and double inputs gives the wrong result.
Description: Scalar min  and max  called with mixed integer and double inputs gives the wrong result. For example, here is a function that finds the minimum of a double and an integer:function x = findMin  x = min(3.5, int8(4)); The minimum is 3.5, which MATLAB should round up to return an integer value of 4. However, when you run this function with the JIT enabled, MATLAB returns an answer of 3:>> findMinans =    3
Workaround: Disable the JIT by entering the following at the MATLAB command prompt:   feature accel off.

Summary: Overflow message in the log when initialization statements involve an unsigned to signed conversion that leads to an overflow on usigned
Description: When, in a initialization statement, there is an unsigned to signed conversion that leads to an overflow on usigned, a message saying that a "certain red error" is detected is displayed in the log. The message will be: test.c:line (rte-red-error) Certain error detected: overflow in constant initializer expression This is an obsolete message, and should be ignored. Example of statement : int x = (int)(unsigned int)-1; Note : This red error does not  appear in the Viewer.
Workaround: You should ignore this message.

Summary: Linearizing a Simulink? model using a fixed-step solver results in error
Description: When you linearize a Simulink? model using a fixed-step solver and both: The linearization result between the input and output point(s) is zero. The fixed-step size is set as a referenced workspace variable.  You may receive this error: Your Simulink model could not be analyzed due to the following error: The value of the "Ts" property must be a real number.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Specify a sample time in the Linearization Options dialog or linoptions object.Switch to a variable-step solver instead of a fixed-step solver in the Simulink? Configuration Parameters.

Summary: Java? for Mac OS? X 10.5 Update 2 software: document bar buttons too big
Description: After installing Java? for OS? X 10.5 Update 2 software, the MATLAB desktop document bar buttons become oversized and have an appearance more suited to isolated buttons.
Workaround: To eliminate the problem from MATLAB? Version 7.7 (R2008b) installations:In the MATLABROOT/java folder, where MATLABROOT is the directory where MATLAB R2008b is installed, create a /patch folder.Download and extract the attached patch, MacDocumentBarPatch2008b.zip, into the /patch folder

Summary: After Java? for Mac OS? X 10.5 Update 2 is installed, fonts in desktop components appear larger, and portions of text may exhibit an odd shifting behavior when selected.
Description: After Java? for Mac OS? X 10.5 Update 2 is installed, fonts in MATLAB desktop components, such as the Command Window, Command History, and Editor, appear larger than usual. Also, text displayed in variable-width fonts, or certain sizes of some monospace fonts, visibly shifts left or right as the selection is extended or reduced, rendering some portions of the text illegible.
Workaround: Apple has acknowledged this problem with Java? for Mac OS? X 10.5 Update 2 and we are working with them to resolve this issue. In the meantime, we have identified two workarounds.The first and simplest workaround is to choose one of the two following specific fonts for the Desktop code font in the Fonts panel of the Preferences dialog:Andale Mono 9Courier New 9These 9-point fonts resemble the prior size and appearance of the default Monospaced 12 font, and do not exhibit the text shifting problem seen with other monospace font variations or with variable-width fonts.The second workaround, which is available only for MATLAB? Version 7.6 (R2008a) or Version 7.7 (R2008b) installations, involves installing a Java patch, which will return all fonts to the correct size, and limit the text-shifting problem to variable-width fonts only, so that any size of any monospace font can be used. (This patch is built in to MATLAB? Version 7.8 (R2009a) and above.) To install the patch:Find the MATLABROOT folder (the directory where MATLAB is installed). For MATLAB? Version 7.7 (R2008b), this folder can be opened by right-clicking or control-clicking on the MATLAB application and selecting "Show Package Contents".Download the appropriate patch, MacFontSizePatch2008a.zip or MacFontSizePatch2008b.zip (see the attachments below).Double-click on the compressed file you just downloaded and it will expand into a folder called "com".Inside the MATLABROOT folder, there is a folder called "java". Inside the java folder, make a new folder called "patch".Move the com folder into the new patch folder.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: smithchart doesn't plot into a subplot
Description: When you use the smithchart  function to plot a Smith chart into a subplot, the function overwrites the figure that contains the subplot with a figure that contains only the Smith chart. The following code shows this behavior: y = (-0.4:0.1:0.5) + 0.2*(ones(1,10))*i; subplot(2,1,1), plot(1:10);subplot(2,1,2), smithchart(y); ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, create the Smith chart in a separate figure, and then resize it and insert it into the subplot. The attached MATLAB files show how to do this:smithchart_subplot.m shows how to include one Smith chart in a figure.smithchart_subplots.m shows how to include multiple Smith charts in a figure.

Summary: Incorrect derivative value for custom equations using Normalize
Description: If you fit a custom equation and use the "Normalize" option (center and scale), you get incorrect results when you call the "differentiate" method.
Workaround: Download the file attachment_494935_20090106.zip that is listed in the Attachments section.Unzip the contents of this file, differentiate.m. Replace your installed version of the file matlab\toolbox\curvefit\curvefit\@cfit\differentiate.m.

Summary: Adding dynamic properties in a class constructor with class attribute ConstructOnLoad set to true may cause MATLAB to crash.
Description: Given a class with the class attribute ConstructOnLoad  set to true, saving an instance of the class and then reloading it invokes the class constructor. In R2008a and R2008b, this may lead to a MATLAB crash if the constructor contains code that adds dynamic properties to the class instance. When a class instance is saved, any dynamic properties added to that instance are also saved. When the instance is reloaded, the dynamic properties are restored. However, if the ConstructOnLoad  attribute is set, the invocation of the class constructor in combination with loading the dynamic properties from the file results in an attempt to add the same dynamic property to the class instance twice. This may cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: If the class attribute ConstructOnLoad must be used, do not add dynamic properties to the class instance in the constructor.

Summary: Documentation incorrect that window choice never affects power spectral density
Description: For modified periodograms and Welch power spectral densities (PSDs), the documentation incorrectly states that calculated average power is independent of the type of window used. When the frequency components are well resolved, the measured average power is generally independent of window choice.

Summary: MATLAB sometimes terminates unexpectly on Windows 32-bit platforms when you use certain tools with their own help pane
Description: When you use certain tools that have their own help pane, such as the Test & Measurement Tool (tmtool ) that is part of the Instrument Control Toolbox, MATLAB sometimes terminates unexpectedly without generating an error log report. This crash occurs only on 32-bit Windows platforms. For example, MATLAB might crash sporadically when creating Interface or Device objects in the tmtool  GUI.
Workaround: Install the attached file, as follows:Determine the matlabroot directory (the directory where MATLAB is installed) by running matlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window.In the matlabroot/java directory, create a patch directory.Download and extract the attached file, HelpPanelCrashPatch.zip, into the patch directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Simulink HDL Coder generates faulty HDL code for the Multiport Switch block from the Simulink Signal Routing library if the input, output, and select ports are all vectors of equal length.
Description: Simulink HDL Coder generates faulty HDL code for the Multiport Switch block from the Simulink Signal Routing library if the input, output, and select ports are all vectors of equal length.
Workaround: To work around this problem, replace the Multiport Switch block with as many Switch blocks as are required to perform the same function as the Multiport Switch block.

Summary: Saving and accessing the State property of a random stream causes a small memory leak.
Description: Saving and accessing the State  property of a random number stream causes a small memory leak. This can affect the system performance when the State  property is saved and restored a large number of times (ca. 10^6 or more, depending on system memory). For example, if the following code, which might be used to reproduce results from the rand  function, were executed a large number of times, you might notice a decrease in available memory: defaultStream = RandStream.getDefaultStream();savedState = defaultStream.State;  r1 = rand(10);defaultStream.State = savedState; r2 = rand(10);
Workaround: Download the file attachment getset_mex.{ARCH}, where {ARCH} is(are) the appropriate mex file suffix(es) for your machine architecture(s). See the mexext function for details about mex file extensions. Install the downloaded file(s) in the {MATLABROOT}/toolbox/matlab/randfun/@RandStream/private directory, where {MATLABROOT} is the directory where MATLAB is installed.Before installing, you should either shut down any MATLAB sessions, or type "clear mex" from within MATLAB.After installing the file, you will need to force MATLAB to update the toolbox path cache file. This can be done using the "Update toolbox cache" option in the Preferences/General dialog or by issuing the command "rehash toolboxcache" at the MATLAB prompt. Type "help toolbox_path_cache" for more information.

Summary: Variable inputs to numerictype constructors in Embedded MATLAB functions result in incomplete error messages
Description: When the numerictype  object constructor is used in an Embedded MATLAB? function, it only accepts constant inputs. If you create a function containing a numerictype  object constructor with a variable (non-constant) input, you will get an error message when you try to compile the function with emlc , emlmex , or the Embedded MATLAB Function block. The error message tries to display the index of the first non-constant input to the numerictype  object constructor, but displays a garbled character instead. For example, in MATLAB?, the following code creates a signed numerictype  object with binary-point scaling, a wordlength of 16 and a fraction length of 13. x = 16;T = numerictype(true,x,13)  If you put this numerictype  object constructor inside a function foo  and try to compile the function using emlc , emlmex , or the Embedded MATLAB Function block, you get an error message that contains a garbled character instead of a valid index value: function t=foo(x)%#emlt = numerictype(true,x,13)   emlc foo -eg {16}  ??? All inputs to this function must be constant. Input #? is not a constant.  In this case, the index of the non-constant input is 2, so the error message should read:??? All inputs to this function must be constant. Input #2 is not constant.  Although there is no workaround to have the correct index displayed in your error message, you can resolve the problem by making sure all of your inputs to the numerictype  object constructor are constant. If an input to the numerictype  object constructor comes from an input to your function, as it does in the previous example, you can make that input constant by using the emlcoder.egc  object. For example, the following code would now compile without error: function t=foo(x)%#eml  t = numerictype(true,x,13)   emlc foo -eg {emlcoder.egc(16)}

Summary: Error when you attempt to import an IVI-COM driver in MIDEDIT
Description: When you import an IVI-COM driver using MIDEDIT, you will encounter an exception that was previously undetected and the editor throws the following error: An error occurred while loading the IVI driver.
Workaround: Back up the file <MATLABROOT>\matlab\toolbox\instrument\instrument\private\privateDeviceConstructorHelper.mSave the attached file as <MATLABROOT>\matlab\toolbox\instrument\instrument\private\privateDeviceConstructorHelper.m

Summary: Function hints for some Filter Design toolbox functions produces an exception in the Command Window
Description: When you try to use function hints for the Filter Design Toolbox functions butter , cheby1 , cheby2 , and ellip , the following exception appears in the Command Window: Exception in thread "Thread-2220" java.lang.StringIndexOutOfBoundsException: String index out of range: -11 at java.lang.String.substring(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.mde.functionhints.MFunctionCall.parseRefPageSignatures(MFunctionCall.java:452) at com.mathworks.mde.functionhints.MFunctionCall.createSignatureString(MFunctionCall.java:183) at com.mathworks.mde.functionhints.FunctionHints$2$1.run(FunctionHints.java:454) at java.lang.Thread.run(Unknown Source) Specifically, when you type butter(  in the Command Window or Editor and wait a few seconds, the exception appears instead of the syntax listing for the function. The problem also happens if you type cheby1( , cheby2( , or ellip( .
Workaround: If you do not want to use function hints, disable them to avoid the exception: Select File > Preferences > Keyboard, and in the preference pane, clear the check boxes Enable in Command Window, and Enable In Editor/Debugger.If you do want to use function hints, download and unzip the attached file to matlabroot/java/patch/com/mathworks/mde/functionhints, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed, which you can determine by running the matlabroot function.If the directory does not exist, create it before downloading the attachments.?

Summary: Stateflow parameters error out when inheriting an enumerated type
Description: Stateflow Parameter data with its type set to "Inherit: Same as Simulink" errors out during model compilation when the parameter value defined in MATLAB has an enumerated data type complaining that the parameter has an unsupported data type.
Workaround: The workaround for this issue is to explicitly set the type of the Stateflow Parameter data to the desired enumerated type. For example, if the enumerated data type name is "BasicColors", the data type should be specified as "Enum: BasicColors" on the data properties dialog box.

Summary: Passing sprintf with no arguments to the error function crashes MATLAB? Version 7.7 (Release 2008b) software
Description: Code such as the following can cause MATLAB? to crash when it is read by the MATLAB Editor: var = sprintf( );...error(var)
Workaround: Passing the results of sprintf with no arguments to the error function is invalid. However, you might enter such code as you are editing a file. The only workaround is to avoid allowing the argument list of sprintf to be empty when you plan to pass the results to another function, such as the error function.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during code generation for models containing multiword block parameters
Description: A segmentation fault occurs during code generation for blocks containing parameters with all of the following conditions:  The block parameter is defined as a variable in the MATLAB work space as a custom storage class. The block parameter is defined as a multiword fixed point data type. The size of the block parameter is larger than or equal to ten.
Workaround: In your model, set the RTWDataReferencesMinSize parameter to infinity using the following command:set_param(0, 'RTWDataReferencesMinSize', inf)Note that this will disable data references.?

Summary: loadobj and saveobj are inherited from superclasses defined using classdef. The product documentation erroneously states that these methods are not inherited.
Description: When a superclass defines a saveobj  or loadobj  method, it is typically not able to save and load parts of the class defined by subclasses. For this reason, a subclass should always define its own implementation of saveobj  and loadobj  if the superclass defines these methods. In future MATLAB versions, either saveobj  or loadobj  might not be inherited, or a warning might be issued if a subclass doesn't define a saveobj  or loadobj  method and it's superclass does.
Workaround: Define saveobj and loadobj methods for any subclass whose superclass(es) define these methods.

Summary: Windows? 64-bit platforms: Help browser links sometimes broken due to MATLAB installation in a directory name containing a space
Description: On Microsoft? Windows? 64-bit platforms, installing MATLAB into a directory whose name contains a space, for example, C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008b , might result in some broken links when you use the Help browser.
Workaround: Install the attached file, as follows:Determine the matlabroot directory (the directory where MATLAB is installed) by running matlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window.In the matlabroot/java directory, create a patch directory.Download and extract the attached file, InstallDirSpacePatch.zip, into the patch directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Document conversion fails when report option is set to increment to prevent overwriting, and the report has an "Import File" component
Description: In the Report Explorer GUI, the top pane of the Report Options section, labelled "Report File Location", contains a checkbox which reads  "If report already exists, increment to prevent overwriting" If this box is checked, and if your report has an "Import File" component,your document conversion may fail. The error message that is displayed is Error reported by XML parserDocument conversion failed    Writing DocBook source    Report complete
Workaround: Backup and replace with attached file  %MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@crg_import_file/execute.p

Summary: Opening the Property Editor for a time series plot created by tstool can freeze MATLAB
Description: Opening the Property Editor for a time series plot from Time Series Tool (tstool ) can cause MATLAB to become unresponsive as a result of a thread deadlock condition. This typically occurs after clicking OK  in response to the question dialog which opens just after creating a time series plot. You create such plots by clicking the Display  button in tstool  or by dragging and dropping the data.
Workaround: If you are using MATLAB 7.3 or later, replace the initialize.m file in the directory:(matlabroot)/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@tsguis/@propeditorwith the initialize.m file in the attached zip archive using the following steps:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\timeseries\@tsguis\@propeditor\initialize.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: initialize.m.oldDownload the attached file, initialize.m storing it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: Cannot print from deployed figures in R2008b on Linux and Mac
Description: When using the printer icon or the Print  menu from a deployed figure on Linux or Mac, the following error messages appear and the figure does not print: ??? Error using ==> printdlg at 25 Could not find toolbox/compiler/deploy on the path.  ??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback  This is an unintended consequence of changes made in R2008b that moved certain MATLAB deployment utility functions from the deployed application's CTF file to the MCR installation kit to enhance the portability of deployed Java applications. However, the bug appears in all deployed modes on Linux and Mac, not only Java applications. The bug does not affect Windows(TM) platforms.
Workaround: A workaround is contained in the attached files printdlg.m (cleartext source) and printdlg.m_enc (encrypted version).These files need only be applied to UNIX, Linux, and Macintosh(TM) installations of MATLAB and MATLAB Compiler Runtime. Windows(TM) installations are not affected by this problem.Do the following to replace the appropriate files in your MATLAB installation:Copy <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler/deploy/printdlg.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler/deploy/printdlg.m_saveCopy <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler/mcr/compiler/printdlg.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler/mcr/compiler/printdlg.m_saveCopy attached printdlg.m to <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler/deploy/printdlg.mCopy attached printdlg.m_enc to <matlabroot>/toolbox/compiler/mcr/compiler/printdlg.mEnsure that the ownership and permissions on the copied files are the same as those of the other MATLAB files in their directory.Do the following to replace the appropriate files in your MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR) installation:Copy <mcrroot>/toolbox/compiler/printdlg.m to <mcrroot>/toolbox/compiler/printdlg.m_saveCopy attached printdlg.m_enc to <mcrroot>/toolbox/compiler/printdlg.mEnsure that the ownership and permissions on the copied file are the same as those of the other MATLAB files in the directory.

Summary: Application lifespan (days) disappears from Configuration Parameters dialog box
Description: The Application lifespan (days)  parameter in the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box might disappear from the user interface when all of the following are true: The system target file is an ERT-based target, such as ert.tlc . The Support non-finite numbers  check box in Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Worksop  >  Interface  pane is cleared.  ?
Workaround: You can specify Application lifespan (days) using set_param at the MATLAB command line. For example, to set Application lifespan (days) to 1, at the MATLAB command line enter: set_param(model_name, 'LifeSpan', 1)

Summary: Compiled Report Generator application fails for RTF and DOC output.
Description: The MATLAB Compiler fails to include several key Report Generator files necessary for RTF and DOC output. When this happens, the Report Generator throws the following error message: Beginning report    Looping on figure "1"    Looping on figure "2"    Converting reportxml.dcl" (No such file or directory)../../../namespace/docbook/v4/dtd/docbookx.dtd:112:17:E: "X20AC" is not a function nametextlink.dsl" (No such file or directory)../../../namespace/docbook/v4/dtd/ent/iso-amsa.ent:8:19:E: "X21B6" is not a function name../../../namespace/docbook/v4/dtd/ent/iso-amsa.ent:9:19:E: "X21B7" is not a function name...../../../namespace/docbook/v4/dtd/dbhierx.mod:1928:48:E: omitted tag minimization parameter can be omitted only if "OMITTAG NO" is specified on the SGML declaration../../../namespace/docbook/v4/dtd/dbhierx.mod:1948:45:E: omitted tag minimization parameter can be omitted only if "OMITTAG NO" is specified on the SGML declaration../../../namespace/mcode/v1/syntaxhighlight.dtd" (No such file or directory)./temp_doc_files/image-001-chapter.xfrag:9:9:E: end tag for "TITLE" omitted, but its declaration does not permit this./temp_doc_files/image-011-chapter.xfrag:9:9:E: end tag for "TITLE" omitted, but its declaration does not permit this./temp_doc_files/image-022-chapter.xfrag:10:9:E: end tag for "TITLE" omitted, but its declaration does not permit this    Converting RTF to DOC    Report complete
Workaround: To workaround this issue, try using the following options with the MCC command:   -p 'rptgen'   -a matlabroot/sys/namespace/mcode/     -a matlabroot/sys/jade/docbook/dtds/      -a matlabroot/sys/jade/docbook/contrib/textlink/

Summary: Errors are reported in Command Window when attempting to zoom in an axes using the zoom tool
Description: While zooming, the following error occasionally appears at the command prompt: ??? Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values. Error in ==> zoom>localWindowButtonDownFcn at 764  if ~isempty(fig_mod) && ... Error in ==> hgfeval at 63  feval(fcn{1},varargin{:},fcn{2:end}); Error in ==> uitools.uimode.modeWindowButtonDownFcn at 81 hgfeval(newButtonDownFcn,hFig,evd); Error in ==> uitools.uimode.modeControl>localModeWindowButtonDownFcn at 133 hThis.modeWindowButtonDownFcn(hFig,evd,hThis,newValue); ??? Error while evaluating figure WindowButtonDownFcn
Workaround: This error occurs when multiple modifier keys (such as control, shift, or alt) are depressed. In such cases, it may be possible to work around this issue by pressing and then releasing the keys while the figure window is active.

Summary: An error dialog is generated when you use the 'Auto Timescale' button on the Timescales tab of the HDL Cosimulation block.
Description: Files required to display an informational dialog when using the Auto Timescale button are missing. Instead of information regarding the timescale chosen by the EDA Simulator Link software, you will get an error dialog that reports that there are missing files: Undefined variable "hdllinkddg" or class"hdllinkddg.AutoTimescaleDialog".  ?

Summary: Simulating a model with multiple input events and Simulink functions inside a Stateflow chart causes a MATLAB crash
Description: During simulation, a MATLAB crash occurs when a Stateflow chart uses the following constructs: More than one input event Simulink functions inside the Stateflow chart  Note that the crash does not occur if there is only one input event.

Summary: Accessing more than 2 GB of data with memmapfile fails when using cell-valued Format field
Description: Attempting to access the Data field of a memmapfile object fails if the Format field is specified as a cell array which represents data that is larger than 2 GB (2^31 bytes). In the following example, the Format represents data that is 2^28 * 4 * 8 (bytes/double) bytes large, or 2^33 bytes. Since 2^33 is larger than 2^31, the assignment to n fails:     m = memmapfile('somefile.dat', 'Format', {'double', [2^28 4], 'x'});   n = m.Data(1).x(1); The error message varies by platform: 64-bit Linux, 64-bit Solaris:    ??? Error using ==> hMapFile   Cannot map file "somefile.dat": Invalid argument. 64-bit Windows:    ??? Error using ==> subsref   Index exceeds matrix dimensions.

Summary: deconvwnr and deconvreg produce incorrect output when input pixel values are scaled up
Description: When input image pixel values are scaled up, say by a factor of 100, deconvwnr  and deconvreg  appear to have no effect; the output image still appears blurred. Pixel-value scaling should not make a difference, but it does.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the versions of the deconvwnr and deconvreg functions in your current installation with the updated versions contained in the attachment, as follows:1. Exit MATLAB. 2. Make backup copies of the following files:matlabroot/toolbox/images/images/deconvwnr.mmatlabroot/toolbox/images/images/deconvreg.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing files, adding "old" to the file extension: deconvwnr.m.OLD and deconvreg.m.OLD. 4. Download the attached file, extract both files, and store them in the directory specified in the previous step. 5. Restart MATLAB. 6. Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Error when saving a Simulink Compensator Design Task
Description: Saving a Simulink Compensator Design Task after modifying the gain using the Graphical Tuning window produces an error.
Workaround: If you are using the R2008b release, please read the following:Extract the attached SCDPatch.zip file into the MATLAB root directory and overwrite the original file.After updating the file, it is recommended that you restart MATLAB and execute the following command:>> rehash toolboxcacheThe SCDPatch.zip file patches both the Control System Toolbox and Simulink Control Design.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when you pass an empty vector to the sound function when running on Apple Macintosh OS X 10.5 operating systems.
Description: Executing the following code in MATLAB can lead to MATLAB crashing on Apple Macintosh OS X 10.5 systems.  Y = [];sound(Y, 48000, 16);
Workaround: To work around this issue, use audioplayer instead of the sound function.Y = [];obj = audioplayer(Y, 48000, 16); playblocking(obj);

Summary: Large matrices stored in the v7.3 MAT-file format sometimes take a long time to load
Description: Loading large matrices from a version 7.3 MAT-file sometimes takes substantially longer than saving to the same file.

Summary: Pasting a large amount of text in to the Variable Editor causes OutOfMemoryError.
Description: Pasting a large amount of text into the Variable Editor can result in a Java OutOfMemoryError . The paste operation fails, and the following appears in the MATLAB Command Window: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: OutOfMemoryError at sun.awt.windows.WClipboard.getClipboardData(Native Method) at sun.awt.datatransfer.ClipboardTransferable.fetchOneFlavor(Unknown Source) at sun.awt.datatransfer.ClipboardTransferable.(Unknown Source) at sun.awt.datatransfer.SunClipboard.getContents(Unknown Source) at com.mathworks.widgets.spreadsheet.SpreadsheetTable.doPaste(SpreadsheetTable.java:1656) ...

Summary: Error downloading HMM profiles from PFAM database using PFAMHMMREAD.
Description: The URL for the PFAM HMM profile repository has changed. This causes the following error when trying to download profiles with the PFAMHMMREAD function. ??? Error using ==> pfamhmmread at 70Input is not a valid PFAM file.Error in ==> getsangerdata at 180        out = pfamhmmread(s);Error in ==> gethmmprof at 82        model = getsangerdata(accessnum,'database','hmm',varargin{:});
Workaround: Follow these steps as a workaround to these changes (last updated September 30, 2008) :1) Quit MATLAB.2) Rename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\private\getsangerdata.m to getsangerdata.m.old (where $MATLAB = the MATLAB root directory on your machine)3) Copy attached file to: getsangerdata.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\private\4) Restart MATLAB.5) After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Changing axes position or size in GUIDE using the 'OuterPosition' property has incorrect results
Description: Resizing or repositioning axes placed using their 'OuterPosition' property in GUIDE places them incorrectly. Using the 'Position' property works correctly, however.

Summary: An instance of stdio.h is added to the list of included files (option -include) each time you launch a verification from Simulink.?
Description: Each time you launch a verification from Simulink (using PolySpace Model Link SL), an additional instance of stdio.h is added to the list of included files in the configuration file. For example, if you launch a verification 20 times, stdio.h will be included in the configuration file 20 times. This does not affect the verification. The only problem is that the configuration file may contain stdio.h multiple times. ?

Summary: Web Services documentation example does not work
Description: An attempt to reproduce the example in the "Using Web Services in MATLAB Applications" documentation at MATLAB > External Interfaces > MATLAB Applications > Web Services generates two errors because the Web service referred to in the documentation has been moved or is no longer available.

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, calling extrinsic functions with at least one structure output may cause compilation errors
Description: In Embedded MATLAB, you may get a compilation error if you call an extrinsic function with multiple outputs when at least one of the outputs is a predefined structure. For example, the following code triggers this bug: x = struct('a',1,'b',2);eml.extrinsic('my_fcn');[x,y] = my_fcn(1); This bug affects Embedded MATLAB functions in Simulink or compiled using emlmex  or emlc .
Workaround: Introduce fresh variables for all of the outputs and reassign them in the next line. For example, you could rewrite the above example as follows:x = struct('a',1,'b',2);eml.extrinsic('my_fcn');[tx,ty] = my_fcn(1);x = tx;y = ty;Variables tx and ty are not used elsewhere in the code.

Summary: gmdistribution.fit method may have an unexpected error message
Description: If one of the components of the estimated mixture distribution has a singular covariance matrix, gmdistribution.fit  has an unexpected error message. For example: >> mu1 = [1 2];>> Sigma1 = [2 0; 0 .5];>> mu2 = [-3 -5];>> Sigma2 = [1 0; 0 1];>> X = [mvnrnd(mu1,Sigma1,1000);mvnrnd(mu2,Sigma2,1000)];   >> X2=[X [ones(1000,1); 2*ones(1000,1)]];   >> gmfit = gmdistribution.fit(X2,2,'covtype','diag');Warning: Failed to converge in 100 iterations for gmdistribution with 2 components > In @gmdistribution\private\gmcluster at 184  In gmdistribution.fit at 199??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.  Error in ==> gmdistribution.fit at 206
Workaround: Change lines 34 and 53 in @gmdistribution/private/wdensity fromif any(L) < eps(max(L))*dtoif any(L < eps(max(L))*d)

Summary: assert failures due to homonymic subprograms
Description: When analyzing Ada code, 3 assert failures may happen during the verification. The following example shows one of these assert failures: ...Verifying one_file ================================================= rte-gnat1 fatal error (Assert_Failure)== Detected at one_file_b.a:3425:23=============================================== N_Procedure_Call_Statement (Node_Id=10787)Rewritten: original node = N_Entry_Call_Statement (Node_Id=343532)Sloc = 161473 one_file_b.a:3425:23Etype = N_Defining_Identifier "any type" (Entity_Id=891s)Name = N_Expanded_Name "_PST95_one_function" (Node_Id=343518)Parameter_Associations = List (List_Id=-99961777)First_Named_Actual = N_Identifier "second_function" (Node_Id=343521) compilation abandoned There is a bug in PolySpace Client for Ada 5.1 (R2008a+). Assert Failures may occur with homonymic subprograms (functions, task entry points), where the first parameters have the same name.
Workaround: To work around the issue, use positional arguments and not named arguments.For example, you can replace:one_package.write(order);with:one_package.write(the data => order);Another workaround would be to change the name of the first parameter to have a unique name for each subprogram. However, this may require many changes in the source files.

Summary: When a diary is active, using the input function in a compiled application does not capture the prompt in the diary.
Description: When a diary is active, using the input  function in a compiled application does not capture the prompt in the diary. ?

Summary: Value class scalar numeric property access slow on 32-bit platforms
Description: The following code executes more slowly when o is an instance of an MCOS value class and Prop is a numeric scalar than when o is a simple struct.     for k=1:1e6      p = o.Prop;   end
Workaround: None

Summary: MinMax Running Resettable block cannot accept parameters as expresssions
Description: The MinMax Running Resettable block is a masked block that has an 'Initial condition' parameter. When the input for this parameter is an expression such as 'a', the following error occurs: Error in 'modelName/MinMax Running Resettable': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated.

Summary: Measurement Computing PCI-DIO24 driver reads incorrect signal values from the board
Description: When accessing digital inputs, the Measurement Computing PCI-DIO24 driver might incorrectly initialize the board and read inaccurate signal values from the board.
Workaround: Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file PCI-DIO24.rwd and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\rtwin\drv\Measurement_Computing.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Deletion of Inport block causes segmentation violation if a duplicate Inport block exists
Description: If an Inport block has a duplicate Inport block associated with it and the original Inport block is deleted, then a segmentation violation occurs.
Workaround: To avoid this error, delete the duplicate Inport block rather than the original Inport block.

Summary: Documentation referring to Windows library support for EDA Simulator Link IN and DS is incorrect
Description: The documentation incorrectly implies that there is support for the HDL simulator running on Windows; however, neither Cadence Incisive nor Synopsys Discovery runs on Windows. Windows support for Incisive and Discovery is on the MATLAB & Simulink side only.
Workaround: There is no software workaround needed. If you are attempting cross-platform cosimulation, refer to the following list of supported libraries when you are copying the EDA Simulator Link DS/IN libraries to the machine with the HDL simulator.Synopsys Discovery and EDA Simulator Link DSThe supported libraries for the Linux32 and Linux64 platforms are:MATLAB Libraryliblfdhdlc_vlog_gcc336.so (default)liblfdhdlc_mixed_gcc336.so (default)liblfdhdlc_vlog_gcc412.soliblfdhldc_mixed_gcc412.soSimulink Libraryliblfdhdls__vlog_gcc336.so (default)liblfdhdls__mixed_gcc336.so (default)liblfdhdls_vlog_gcc412.soliblfdhdls_mixed_gcc412.soCadence Incisive and EDA Simulator Link INThe supported libraries for the Linux32 and Linux64 platforms are:MATLAB Libraryliblfihdlc_gcc323.so (default for IUS 6.1 or unsupported releases)liblfihdlc_gcc346.soliblfihdlc_gcc41.so (default for IS 6.2, 8.1)liblfihdlc_tmwgcc.soSimulink Libraryliblfihdls_gcc323.so (default for IUS 6.1 or unsupported releases)libflihdls_gcc346.soliblfihdls_gcc41.so (default for IUS 6.2, 8.1)liblfihdsl_tmwgcc.soThe supported libraries for the Solaris32 and Solaris64 platforms are:MATLAB Libraryliblfihdlc_gcc323.so (default for IUS 6.1 or unsupported releases)liblfihdlc_gcc41.so (default for IS 6.2, 8.1)liblfihdlc_tmwspro.soSimulink Libraryliblfihdls_gcc323.so (default for IUS 6.1 or unsupported releases)liblfihdls_gcc41.so (default for IS 6.2, 8.1)liblfihdls_tmwspro.so

Summary: MATLAB stack trace not printed for standalone application in release R2008a
Description: Standalones rely on the MCR to print error messages with MATLAB code stack traces. Beginning in release R2008a, if there is error in a standalone applicaiton, the stack trace is not printed.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: code execution result for enabled and triggered subsystem does not match simulation result
Description: The build process might generate incorrect code if both of the following conditions apply to your model: The model contains an enabled and triggered subsystem. The trigger signal has two consecutive falling edges or two consecutive rising edges.  The simulation correctly ingores the second of two consecutive edges, but the generated code does not.

Summary: Segmentation violation during code generation when Stateflow chart connects to Merge or Matrix Concatenate block
Description: A segmentation violation might occur when generating code with the Real-Time Workshop software for a model containing a Stateflow chart under the following conditions: The Stateflow chart connects to a Merge block and the block parameter Input port offsets  is specified, or the Stateflow chart connects to a Matrix Concatenate block and the block parameter Concatenate dimension  is set to 2 . The output data to Simulink in the Stateflow chart is used in an index expression, for example, a[output_data].  The attached model sf_merge_example.mdl  illustrates this segmentation violation. ?
Workaround: To prevent the segmentation violation, copy the output data from the Stateflow chart into a local variable and use the local variable in the index expression.In the attached zip file, the model sf_merge_example.mdl contains a Stateflow chart "Chart with Workaround" which illustrates this workaround.

Summary: LCC fails to compile C source files that do not terminate with a newline character
Description: The LCC compiler requires that all C source files terminate with a newline character. Therefore, if you use LCC as your MEX compiler, you must add a newline character to the end of all custom C files that you compile: With Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts or Simulink models With Stateflow charts Using the emlc  command.  Otherwise, you get a compiler error.
Workaround: Ensure that all custom C source code files have a terminating newline character.

Summary: Models that contain Analog Output blocks, using the Measurement Computing PCI-DDA-08/16 board, might not produce analog output
Description: Models that contain Analog Output blocks, using the Measurement Computing PCI-DDA-08/16 board, might not produce any voltage on analog outputs. This is due to resource initialization problems in the driver.
Workaround: As a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file PCI-DDA08-16.rwd and save it to <matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\rtwin\drv\Measurement_ComputingRestart MATLAB.

Summary: PolySpace displays useless preprocessor warnings about nested comments during compilation phase
Description: During the compilation checking phase, PolySpace displays many warning messages about nested comments. For example: ...Verifying myfile.cmyfile.c:20: warning: nested comment is not allowedmyfile.c:21: warning: nested comment is not allowedmyfile.c:<#line>: warning: nested comment is not allowed...  Theses messages are useless unless you enable MISRA-C rule 2.3.

Summary: Error when saving a SISO Design Tool session
Description: Saving a SISO Design Tool session after modifying the gain using the Graphical Tuning window produces an error.
Workaround: If you are using the R2008b release, please read the following:Extract the attached CSTPatch.zip file into the MATLAB root directory and overwrite the original file.After updating the file, it is recommended that you restart MATLAB and execute the following command:>> rehash toolboxcacheIf you have a license for Simulink Control Design, extract the attached SCDPatch.zip file into the MATLAB root directory and overwrite the original file. The SCDPatch.zip file patches both the Control System Toolbox and Simulink Control Design.

Summary: emlc and emlmex may produce build errors when you use LCC to compile MATLAB files with fixed-point types greater that 32 bits
Description: emlc  and emlmex  produce build errors when you use LCC to compile MATLAB files with fixed-point types greater than 32 bits if you have a command called sh  on your Windows path. To see if sh  is on your path, follow these steps: Open a command window From the Start menu, select Run  and enter cmd . Type sh  and press Enter . If you get a message indicating that sh  is not found, then it is not on your path.
Workaround: Run MATLAB in an environment where the sh command is not on the Windows path. Please refer to Windows documentation for a description of how to change your Windows path.

Summary: Microsoft Excel software crashes when using the PolySpace_Macros.xls Excel MACRO on Japanese OS Windows machines
Description: PolySpace_Macros.xls crashes when processing files located in the PolySpace-Doc folder containing Japanese double byte characters in the comment field. A Windows error message appears whether or not the files contain Japanese double byte characters.
Workaround: Note that following workaround is only available for release R2008b.To workaround this issue under Linux: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:$POLYSPACE_C/lib/PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Linux or Solaris machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_490019_client_R2008b_linux_2008-11-28.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (/usr/local/Polyspace by default under Linux) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacementsTo workaround this issue under Windows: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\lib\PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Windows machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_490019_client_R2008b_win_2008-11-28.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (C:\Polyspace by default under Windows) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacementsTo generate reports for existing PolySpace results, copy the attached file (PolySpace_Macros.xls) to the 'PolySpace-Doc' folder of each existing results directory.Note that the PolySpace_Macros.xls in attachment does not work with MS Office 2007.

Summary: Help command for mech_import does not display any help text
Description: Entering help mech_import  at the MATLAB command line does not display any help text.
Workaround: There are two workarounds for this issue.Enter doc mech_import at the MATLAB command line. The Help browser opens at the documentation for the mech_import command.Download and place the attached mech_import.m file in the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/mech/importer/ directory, where $MATLABROOT is the directory returned by the command matlabroot. Then enter the command rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: FINDPEAKS may skip single peaks when using the 'MinPeakHeight' option.
Description: If the data has a single peak with less than three data points above the specified 'MinPeakHeight', FINDPEAKS  will ignore the peak. For example, a = [0 0 3 0 0];findpeaks(a,'MinPeakHeight',2)  ans =  []

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code generated for model reference simulation target can fail to compile when model calls submodel that specifies custom function prototypes
Description: The code generated for a model reference simulation target can fail to compile when a parent model calls a submodel that specifies custom function prototypes using the function prototype control interface. Custom function prototypes do not apply to simulation targets and should be disregarded when generating code for a model reference simulation target. In this case, the parent model erroneously tries to call the customized step function of the submodel, which causes "undefined reference " errors at compile time.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform either of the following actions:Change the simulation mode of the block referencing the submodel from Accelerator to Normal. However, this workaround does not apply to models that permit multiple instances.Temporarily clear the custom function prototype specification of the submodel when generating simulation target code for the parent model.

Summary: The 'Table Data' property of the Look-up Table of a model saved as MATLAB 6.5.1 (R13SP1) using MATLAB 7.5 (R2007b) appears different from the same model opened directly in MATLAB 6.5.1 (R13SP1)
Description: Simulink does not preserve the Table Data for the Look-up Table block when a model in R2008b-R2007b is saved to R2007a or earlier. When opening the model in the earlier release, the OutputValue parameter is set to the default value.
Workaround: 1) Save the Table Data to a .mat file.2) In the older version of Simulink, load the Table Data from the .mat file.3) Set the matrix of Table Data to the values used in the newer version of Simulink.

Summary: Failure to deallocate storage in function ishghandle can cause MATLAB to run out of memory
Description: By failing to release allocated memory, the ishghandle  function causes the size of MATLAB memory to gradually increase. The following code snippet illustrates the issue: clearclose allwhile true    for i=1:100        ishghandle(1);    end        v = memory;    fprintf('Memory used by MATLAB: %d MB\n', ...        floor(v.MemUsedMATLAB/1024/1024));end
Workaround: To work around this issue download the attached file, ishghandle.m, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Download the attached file, ishghandle.m storing it in any local directory (For example, C:\utils).Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.Add C:\utils to the MATLAB path using the addpath function in MATLAB: addpath('C:\utils')Ensure that the command which -all ishghandle points to the newly saved function in C:\utils. Verify that the memory leak is fixed by running the reproduction steps described above.

Summary: Editing a Data Store Memory block after model simulation can cause a system crash
Description: When you simulate a model that contains a Data Store Memory block, then edit the block, a system crash can occur if the block does all of the following: Uses a Signal object. Specifies a Minimum  and/or Maximum  value. Inherits its data type (Data type  is Inherit: auto ).  This problem does not occur when you edit a Data Store Memory block before simulating a model for the first time.
Workaround: Make at least one of the following changes to the Data Store Memory block:Do not assign a Signal object to the block.Do not specify either a Minimum or Maximum value.Specify the block's data type explicitly rather than inheriting it.

Summary: Right-click build fails on subsystem fed by duplicate inports
Description: Right-click build for a subsystem might fail under all of the following conditions: A duplicated Inport block of a subsystem has a Bus Selector block as its source The duplicated Inport block connects to a Bus Creator block within the subsystem A bus element is selected from this new bus within the subsystem  ?

Summary: Entity Combiner block works incorrectly in some situations involving Event-Based Entity Generator block
Description: The Entity Combiner block performs too few combining operations when all of the following conditions are true: An entity input port of the Entity Combiner block connects to an Event-Based Entity Generator block, with no server blocks in between. The Event-Based Entity Generator block generates more than two entities simultaneously. The Resolve simultaneous signal updates according to event priority  checkbox is not selected in the Event-Based Entity Generator block.
Workaround: Select the checkbox labeled Resolve simultaneous signal updates according to event priority in the Event-Based Entity Generator block. Configure the Event priority parameter to derive the desired event execution precedence.

Summary: Continuous-time Stateflow charts interpret comments incorrectly as condition actions
Description: In continuous-time Stateflow charts, you cannot add condition actions to intermediate transitions , which are transitions that do not directly reach a state. This restriction prevents actions that have side-effects from being performed in minor time steps. Violations cause errors during simulation. Adding comments to intermediate transitions in continuous-time charts also causes runtime errors because Stateflow incorrectly interprets comments as condition actions. For example, if you try to simulate the model example1.mdl  in the attached ZIP file, you will get an error.
Workaround: In continuous-time charts, add comments only on transitions that connect directly to a state, as in example2.mdl in the attached ZIP file.

Summary: When using DRS with the HC12 target, PolySpace software crashes with message: "Fatal error : Lack of cast between incompatible result type"?
Description: When using DRS with the HC12 target, you can have a problem in the C to IL phase. The crash will look like: *** C to intermediate language translation 3 (P_SIA)Fatal error : Lack of cast between incompatible result type and second operandtype in PROC P.Pst_Global_Assert_1instr : 55: mul t:g4 (signed int.32) Pst_Global_Assert_1/1.r4 e1, e2  ["source coordinates"  "?pst_hidden_file?" 1 1] 56: e1: ldc t:g4 (signed int.322  ["source coordinates"  "?pst_hidden_file?"  1  1]  57: e2: ldc t:g3 (signed int.16 0   ["source coordinates"  "?pst_hidden_file?"  1  1]**** C to intermediate language translation 3 (P_SIA) took 0real, 0u + 0s----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: 1 error(s) detected by the SUIF checkerAn internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,the computation has been halted.Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support sitehttps://www.mathworks.com/support.Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.----------------------------------------------------------------------  ---------------------------------------------------------------------- ------ Verifier has encountered an internal error. ------ Please contact your technical support. ------ ----------------------------------------------------------------------  Example : --- source file ----int x[3] = { 0, 1, 2};int main() {return x[2];} ---- drs file ---x -10 200 globalassert ?
Workaround: Many problems could lead to same message. To avoid this issue, do one of the following depending of initial context:If target is a 16-Bits target, issue would disappear by changing the target. For example, use a c-167 target instead of hc12.If DRS file has been used, add the qualifier static to the variable used in the DRS file.If -retype-pointer option has been used, issue would disappear by relaunching without option.?

Summary: lookfor displays code instead of links to help when running in -nodesktop mode
Description: lookfor  normally displays links to help for all of the functions it finds. But when you start MATLAB with the -nodesktop  startup option, lookfor  displays the link code instead of the link. This is because links are not supported in -nodesktop  mode.

Summary: gen-HTML-files does not handle Japanese characters properly
Description: After generating HTML results from a results directory using the gen-HTML-files command, Japaneses characters in comments are not properly displayed in the HTML browser.

Summary: The network boot functionality fails to establish communication between host PC and target PC
Description: The network boot functionality fails to establish communication between the host PC and target PC. The target PC displays messages like the following on the target PC monitor. TFTP....PXE-E32 TFTP open timeoutPXE-E32 TFTP open timeoutPXE-MOF exiting Intel PXE The use of Ethernet cards that do not support 1500 byte transfer block sizes causes this issue. For example, the National Semiconductor DP83815 chip used on the Diamond Systems Athena II board causes this issue.
Workaround: As a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Start the Windows Task Manager and stop the xpctftpserver process.Download the attached file, xpctftpserver.exe, and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\bin\xpctftpserver.exeRestart MATLAB.Restart the xpctftpserver process.

Summary: Creating an IDDATA object with unequal sampling times produces an error.
Description: An error occurs when you use the IDDATA constructor to create an IDDATA object with an irregularly sampled time vector: data = iddata(output, input, [], timeVector) Note: Most commands in System Identification Toolbox software require regularly sampled data. For IDDATA objects with an irregular time vector, you can only plot and detrend the data; you cannot use other data processing and model estimation commands.
Workaround: Use these steps to create an IDDATA object with unequal sampling time intervals:Create an IDDATA object with equal sampling time intervals. Use any positive scalar to set a sampling time Ts.data = iddata(y, u, Ts)Create a variable that stores the desired time instants. For exampe, TimeVector.Assign the SamplingInstants property of data to TimeVector:data.SamplingInstants = TimeVector;

Summary: The last row of a timeseries object sometimes does not display in the Variable Editor.
Description: If a timeseries object has 200*n+1 (where n is an integer) the last row of the timeseries will not be populated in the Variable Editor until some operation is performed at the command line.
Workaround: Copy the file UpdateArrayEditorTableCache.m from the attached zip archive into the following directory:(matlabroot)/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@tsguisfor MATLAB 7.3 and beyond. You will need to restart MATLAB to see the effect.

Summary: If the 280x/2833x ePWM "Timer period source" is set to "Input port", the block doesn't pass the "Timer initial period" value to the generated code.
Description: If the 280x/2833x ePWM "Timer period source" is set to "Input port", the block doesn't pass the "Timer initial period" value to the generated code.
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, please read the following:Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_489290.zip corresponding to the MATLAB version you want to patch. This patch is available for MATLAB releases R2008a and R2008b. Start MATLAB and type "ver" at the prompt to verify which version of MATLAB you are using. Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt to install the patch:For MATLAB release R2008a:patch_installer('patch_489290_R2008a.zip', 'install')For MATLAB release R2008b:patch_installer('patch_489290_R2008b.zip', 'install')

Summary: Models containing the Generic Power System Stabilizer or Multi-Band Power System Stabilizer blocks cannot run when the Control System ToolboxTM software is not installed
Description: The Generic Power System Stabilizer and Multi-Band Power System Stabilizer blocks are using functionality of the Control System Toolbox in their internal modeling. If you do not have a Control System Toolbox license, trying to simulate a model containing these blocks produces an error about undefined function or variable 'tf'.

Summary: In a figure containing subplots with multiple grids, selecting a single axes using the subplot function causes the axes to reset if a legend or colorbar has been added to the axes.
Description: If multiple subplots have been created with different grid sizes and a legend or colorbar has been added, as in the following example:figure subplot(1,2,2) plot([1:10])subplot(2,2,3) plot([1:10]) colorbar Attempting to select a subplot using the syntaxsubplot(2,2,3) Will cause the specified subplot axes to reset.
Workaround: To work around this issue, save a handle to the subplot. In order to select the specified subplot, use the axes command as in the following example:figure ax1 = subplot(1,2,2) plot([1:10])ax2 = subplot(2,2,3) plot([1:10]) colorbaraxes(ax2);

Summary: Simscape language is not case sensitive for model member names; results in 'Property name already in use' error message
Description: Simscape language is not case sensitive for member names. If two member names differ only in case, then you will receive an error 'Property name already in use ' when running ssc_build .
Workaround: Give members unique names irrespective of case.

Summary: Resource configuration error when TOUCAN Receive block is configured to use interrupt-driven FIFO queue for received messages buffer
Description: You can configure the TouCAN Receive block to use an interrupt-driven FIFO queue to buffer received messages (by selecting the Use interrupt driven FIFO queue to buffer received messages  check box in the Source Block Parameters  dialog box). However, a resource configuration error is generated when you do this.
Workaround: When the Use interrupt driven FIFOqueue to buffer received messages check box is selected, the initialization code tries to acquire resources for the block. Some of this code is not required and must be removed:In the Command Window, enter "mpc555toucan". A window appears with the mpc555toucan library blocks.Select Edit > Unlock Library.Select and right-click TouCAN Receive.Select Edit Mask. The Mask Editor appears.Select Initialization.From the Initialization commands window, remove the following section of redundant code:if use_queue% Perform Resource Configurationresource_type = sprintf('CAN_%s_IRQS', get_param(gcb,'module'));resource = sprintf('CAN_IRQ_BUFFER%d',bufferids);RTWConfiguration.Target.acquireResourceForBlock( ...gcb, ... % Current block'MPC555dkConfig.TOUCAN', ... % Configuration Classresource_type, ... % Resource Type associated with the configuration classresource, ... % Resource being requested'CAN Interrupt' ... % Lable to be used in error messages);end7. Click OK.?

Summary: When using gen-HTML-files.exe the following error appears: 'Exception in thread "main" java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/polyspace/iabc/1/Root '
Description: When trying to generate results in HTML format using gen-HTML-files.exe  located in %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\wbin , an error occurs with the following message: Exception in thread "main" java.lang.NoClassDefFoundError: com/polyspace/iabc/1/Root
Workaround: Use gen-HTML-files.exe located in %POLYSPACE_COMMON%\Viewer\wbin

Summary: The FTP DIR command throws a Java exception when the server is running less common software.
Description: The FTP protocol's DIR command does not conform to any specification. Because the reply is meant to be understood by humans and not other programs, it can be difficult to process the information in a reliable way. If the FTP server software uses an uncommon reply format, this can crash the code that parses the response to extract information like filename, file size, and timestamp.
Workaround: In toolbox/matlab/iofun/@ftp/dir.m, replace lines 31 through 51:remoteFileList=ftp.listFiles(str);...endwithlisting=[];This returns the filenames without attempting to determine the other metadata.

Summary: Function textm is inconsistent with the MATLAB text function given a single point location and a cell array of text strings
Description: Given a single point location and a cell array of text strings, text  constructs a multi-line text object, with each string on its own line. But if you give scalar latitude-longitude input and a (non-scalar) cell array of text strings to textm , you'll receive the following error message: "Size of argument STRING is inconsistent with sizes of coordinate arrays." In addition, if you provide a single point location and an empty text string, textm  generates an error, and may possibly generate a segmentation violation as well.
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the version of function textm.m in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/textm.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: textm.m.oldDownload the attached file, textm.m, storing it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: Waitbar fails to execute when the user sets its UserData property
Description: The waitbar  GUI function fails to execute when its user sets its UserData  property. This occurs as a side effect of storing information about the waitbar's current state, which the function does to achieve more efficient performance.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the waitbar.m function in your current installation with the updated version attached, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/itools/waitbar.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, waitbar.m.OLD.Download the attached file, waitbar.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.This version of waitbar allows users to set UserData on the wait bar without causing any MATLAB errors.

Summary: Using the Save button icon in the toolbar of Powergui FFT Analysis tool corrupts the tool
Description: When you click the Save  button in the toolbar of the FFT Analysis tool, MATLAB saves the figure using this default file name and file location: matlab/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/power_fftscope.fig  In doing so, and if your MATLAB path directories are not write protected, you overwrite a SimPowerSystems source code file. This overwrite corrupts the FFT Analysis tool and you will no longer be able to use the tool, until you reinstall the original power_fftscope.fig  source file.
Workaround: Until this issue is fixed, you must not use the Save button icon to save the FFT plot results. Instead, use the Save as option of the File menu, saving the figure using a different name and file location than the default SimPowersystems file.If you are using R2008a or later, you can fix the FFT Analysis tool by downloading the attached file power_fftscope.fig.Copy it in the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.?

Summary: Bessel functions with NaN inputs cause a segmentation fault on 64-bit Linux and Windows.
Description: Using the besselk  or besselj  function with NaN values, i.e., besselk(NaN, NaN)  causes a segmentation fault on 64-bit Linux and Windows.

Summary: PolySpace for Ada can generate useless checks related to initialization procedures of complex types
Description: In order to simulate the initialization of variables of complex types, such as records or arrays, PolySpace for Ada creates initialization procedures called _init_proc  for these types. These procedures are then called in contexts where the initialization of those variables is needed. In some cases, checks resulting from the verification of these initialization procedures are reported. Since these checks come from internally generated code, they usually do not provide any useful information and should be ignored. In main-generator mode, these checks are green. However, if you specify a main subprogram using the -main  option, the initialization procedure might not be called and these checks will be grey. ?

Summary: Unit definitions of the BTU, metric horsepower, and one atmosphere are not compliant with the NIST guidelines for use of SI units
Description: The definitions of the BTU, metric horsepower, and one atmosphere specified in the Simscape unit manager differ slightly from the values compliant with the NIST guidelines for use of SI units. The differences are as follows: - The BTU is rounded to a value of 1055.0558. It should be consistent with the international calorie defined as "cal_2_J*1000*lbm_2_kg*5/9 = 1055.05585262", where cal_2_J is 4.1868 J/cal and lbm_2_kg is 0.45359237 kg/lbm. - The Metric Horsepower is defined as 735.4996249 Watt. It should be changed to 735.49875 Watt. - One atmosphere is defined as 101325.027 Pa. It should be changed to 101325 Pa.
Workaround: To work around this issue:1. Download the attached file pm_units.m.2. Depending on your version of Simscape, copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:- For V2.1(R2008a) and V3.0(R2008b)$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/data_manager/data_manager/- For earlier versions$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_util/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.?

Summary: Why does the fmincon solver get stuck at a point where my objective function is far from optimal, and possibly very large?
Description: The fmincon  active-set (formerly known as 'medium-scale'), fminimax , fgoalattain , and fseminf  solvers use a flawed line-search termination criterion. This flawed criterion does not let the solver perform a line-search when the objective or constraint function values, or their gradients, are very large or poorly scaled. The solver then cannot make any progress.

Summary: Mask callback errors when selecting Signal Variation parameters in Fuel Cell Stack block
Description: The Fuel Cell Stack block in the Electric Drives library shows the following error message when you select items in the Signal Variation tab of the mask: --> Attempt to modify block in a linked subsystem. This can only be done by the block or its parent through their mask initialization code. --> Error evaluating 'MaskCallback' callback of Fuel Cell Stack block (mask) 'Fuel Cell Stack'. Attempt to modify block in a linked subsystem. This can only be done by the block or its parent through their mask initialization code.  Note that if you click the Apply  button, input ports representing the selected control signals are correctly added to the block. The model is then working fine.
Workaround: To eliminate the error message:1. Download the attached file electricdrivelib.mdl.2. Copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/drives/drives/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Warning about wrong release when you create data acquisition objects
Description: When you create an analoginput, analogoutput or digitalio object you may see the following warning even when you use a third party adaptor that is not tied to a specific release of MATLAB: Warning: The device adaptor is not in the expected location.Expected: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2008b\matlabActual: C:\Program Files\CompanyX\Adaptor\Matlab\deviceAdaptor.dllAn adaptor from the wrong release may be in use. To correct this problem execute:  daqregister('deviceAdaptor','unload')Then exit and restart MATLAB.  This is due to a problem in the code that checks if the correct version of adaptors provided with the Data Acquisition Toolbox is in use. It also checks for third party adaptors (those provided by companies other than The MathWorks) when it should not be doing so.
Workaround: To work around this problem execute these commands in the MATLAB window:warning('off', 'daq:analoginput:adaptormismatch')warning('off', 'daq:analogoutput:adaptormismatch')warning('off', 'daq:digitalio:adaptormismatch')?

Summary: emlc and emlmex might fail to compile MATLAB code that contains persistent variables named y0, y1, or yn.
Description: emlc  and emlmex  might fail to compile MATLAB code that contains persistent variables named y0 , y1 , or yn  because these names clash with standard function names in the C library header file math.h . The error message looks like this: Build error: Compilation returned error status code... The error occurs if variables y0 , y1 , or yn  appear in generated code and the generated code uses math.h .
Workaround: If you use a version of emlmex or emlc earlier than R2009a, rename your persistent variables so they do not clash with names of functions in standard C libraries. Avoid y0, y1, yn, along with other conflicting names such as sin, strlen, or printf.Alternatively, if you use version R2009a or later of emlc with Real-Time Workshop or Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder, you can choose naming rules for all generated variables using the emlcoder.RTWConfig object.

Summary: Incorrect Environment Controller output during Simulink Design Verifier analysis
Description: During Simulink Design Verifier analysis, an Environment Controller block incorrectly passes the RTW port input instead of the Sim port input to its output. Results for models that contain Environment Controller blocks may be invalid after analysis.
Workaround: Please perform the following steps to fix this bug in R2008a:Move matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\simulink.mdl to a different folder, one that is outside the MATLAB path.Copy simulink_8a.mdl to matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks and rename simulink_8a.mdl to simulink.mdl.Copy sldvisactive_8a.p to matlabroot\toolbox\sldv\sldv and rename sldvisactive_8a.p to sldvisactive.p.Restart MATLAB.Please perform the following steps to fix this bug in R2008b:Move matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks\simulink.mdl to a different folder, one that is outside the MATLAB path.Copy simulink_8b.mdl to matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\blocks and rename simulink_8b.mdl to simulink.mdl.Copy sldvisactive_8b.p to matlabroot\toolbox\shared\sldv and rename sldvisactive_8b.p to sldvisactive.p.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Defining the initial value of a property as an anonymous function handle prevents the first constructed object from being cleared, and prevents the class itself from being cleared.
Description: If a class defines a property initial value to be an anoymous function, as in the following code: classdef MyClass  properties    fh = @(x,y)x+y;  endend  Then, the first instance of the above class can never be removed from memory and the class itself cannot be cleared. ?
Workaround: You can avoid this problem by assigning the function handle inside the constructor, instead of defining an initial value for the property:classdef MyClass  properties    fh;  end  methods    function obj = MyClass       obj.fh = @()x+y;    end  endend?

Summary: Error simulating an open-circuit Series RLC Branch by setting R=0, L=0, and C = inf
Description: According to the documentation of the Series RLC Branch block you can force an open circuit by setting R and L to 0, and C to inf . The block works fine if you type 0,0, and inf , respectively, into the mask of the block. However, if you define variable names in the mask of the block, for example: 'R','L', and 'C', and then set R=0;L=0;C=inf;  at command line, you get an error message when you try to simulate your model: In mask of 'Series RLC Branch' block: A minimum of one of R, L, or C parameters must be defined.
Workaround: To define an open-circuit branch, you must set the Branch type parameter to Open circuit.

Summary: Structures containing numerictype or fimath fields may cause assertions during Embedded MATLAB compilation
Description: Structures defined locally in Embedded MATLAB functions may cause compilation to abort in the following circumstances: You create the structure locally using the struct() constructor, as in this example: v = struct('field1', numerictype(), 'field2', 3.14); You initialize one or more structure fields with a fimath  or numerictype  object. You assign constants (known at compile time) to all other structure fields. The structure is assigned to a persistent variable, returned from a function, passed to a subfunction, or passed to a C function.  When you encounter this bug, the error message looks like this: assertion failed: const_to_string: unknown constant type at line 1226 of file "dom_c/cg_ast.cpp"  This bug affects Embedded MATLAB functions used in Simulink, and compiled with emlc  and emlmex  command-line tools.
Workaround: Remove the numerictype or fimath field from the structure, or add a new field that contains a value that cannot be known at the compile time, such as a block input.

Summary: Models driven by external interrupts run multiple times at each interrupt
Description: If you stop and immediately restart models driven by external interrupts, those models might run multiple times at each interrupt. This means that these models run two or more times faster than the interrupt rate.
Workaround: As a workaround, when you stop a model driven by an external interrupt, wait two or three seconds before restarting the model. This wait enables the kernel to clean up the remnants from the last run before the next run.

Summary: When certain SimDriveline demos are run in Rapid Accelerator mode, lasterror set to error message referring to pm_productsindevelopment function
Description: When certain SimDriveline demos are run in Rapid Accelerator mode, the error variable lasterror  is set to this error message: Undefined function or variable 'pm_productsindevelopment' These demos include drive_4wd_dynamics, for example.
Workaround: Rename the file $MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\pm_sli\pm_sli\@PmSli\@RunTimeModule\getProductLicenses.p to getProductLicenses.p.old.($MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory obtained by entering the command matlabroot at the MATLAB command propmpt.)Down the attached getProductLicenses.p file and place it in the directory $MATLABROOT\toolbox\physmod\pm_sli\pm_sli\@PmSli\.Restart MATLAB and etner the following commands at the MATLAB command prompt:clear allclear classesrehash toolboxcache

Summary: Unable to run or save Simulink model with Simscape blocks when the active configuration set is a Configuration Set reference
Description: The following error is reported when you try to run or save a model with Simscape blocks when the active configuration set is a Configuration Set reference: Warning: Error evaluating 'PreSaveFcn' callback of 2-Position ValveActuator block (mask) 'mymodel/2-Position Valve Actuator'. No method 'attachToConfigSet' withmatching signature found for class 'SSC.SimscapeCC'.Warning: No method 'attachToConfigSet' with matching signature found for class'SSC.SimscapeCC'.Warning: Error evaluating 'PostSaveFcn' callback of 2-Position ValveActuator block (mask) 'mymodel/2-Position Valve Actuator'. No method 'attachToConfigSet' withmatching signature found for class 'SSC.SimscapeCC'.

Summary: A parent model that references a model that contains a bus signal marked for logging may fail to update, simulate, or log all data
Description: When a model contains a Model block, the model may fail to update, simulate, or log all data marked for logging if: The referenced model executes in Accelerator mode. A bus signal in the referenced model is marked for logging. The output port from which the bus signal originates is not the first output of the block.  The problem that occurs depends on whether the bus signal marked for logging is virtual or nonvirtual. If the bus signal is virtual, model update and simulation will succeed, but no data will be logged for the bus signal. Simulink may issue a spurious warning message similar to the following: Warning: Cannot log signal with path 'model_name/block_name ' and port index 2 in 'parent_model/model_ref_block '.
