The most likely cause is that a selected test pointed signal is not loggable, or the test points in the referenced model have been changed. To find out whether a test pointed signal is not loggable, open the referenced model, use the Signal Properties dialog to configure the signal to log data, and simulate the referenced model. If the signal is not loggable, a descriptive error message will appear.   If the bus signal is nonvirtual, model update and simulation may fail even though no error exists in the model, and Simulink may issue a spurious error message similar to the following: Data type 'BusObject ' for block 'model_name/block_name ', output port 2 in model 'model_name ' is invalid.  Custom data types are not supported when logging signals in referenced models; data types must be builtin, or fixed-point.  The data type name in the error message refers to the name of the Bus Object that defines the nonvirtual bus.
Workaround: Use a Signal Conversion block in Bus Copy mode to copy the bus signal that is to be logged.Log the output of the Signal Conversion block.

Summary: MEX errors when using the LCC compiler and -outdir points to the current working directory which is a mapped network drive.
Description: MEX throws the following error if -outdir  is the current working directory and that directory is a mapped network drive, and you are using the LCC compiler: >> mex('-outdir', pwd, fullfile(matlabroot,'extern','examples','mex','yprime.c')) U:\MATLAB\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Rename of 'H:\TEMP\MEX\ASDFGH~1\yprime.mexw32' failed: No such file or directory ??? Error using ==> mex at 213Unable to complete successfully. If you are using the verbose , -v , option you may also see the following text before the error message: Writing library for yprime.mexw32 Manually moving yprime.mexw32 to H:\TEMP\MEX\ASDFGH~1\yprime.mexw32 Renaming yprime.mexw32 to H:\TEMP\MEX\ASDFGH~1\yprime.mexw32 U:\MATLAB\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Rename of 'H:\TEMP\MEX\ASDFGH~1\yprime.mexw32' failed: No such file or directory
Workaround: To work around this issue, download and install the attached files. This patch applies only to MATLAB version 7.7 (R2008b).Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/bin/mex.pl to matlabroot/bin/mex.pl_old.Download the attached ZIP archive mex_486734.zip.Unzip mex.pl from the archive file to matlabroot/bin/mex.pl.

Summary: Code generation for model reference target can fail with "index out of range" message when referenced model uses function prototype control
Description: During a build of a model containing a model reference hierarchy, code generation for a model reference target can fail if the referenced model uses function prototype control. The software reports an error similar to the following during TLC processing: Error: File: matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/codeinfomdlreflib.tlc Line: 254 Column: 60The specified index (4) was out of the range 0 - 3Error: File: matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/codeinfomdlreflib.tlc Line: 255 Column: 36Ambiguous reference to ci - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopesError: File: matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/codeinfomdlreflib.tlc Line: 255 Column: 36The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2008b, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name: matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/codeinfomdlreflib.tlcDownload the attached file and place it at the same location as the file you renamed.Restart MATLAB and rebuild your model.

Summary: Documentation for "Convolutional Coding: Specifying a Trellis in MATLAB" incorrectly states that outputs should be decimal numbers
Description: The documentation section "Specifying a Trellis in MATLAB" for Convolutional codes in the Communications Toolbox states that the outputs field of the trellis structure should be decimal numbers. The outputs field should be in octal numbers.

Summary: Why do I get an error when running a SystemTest test harness generated from Simulink Design Verifier?
Description: I get the following error message in the Run Status when I run a SystemTest test harness generated from Simulink Design Verifier: No appropriate method, property, or field Time for class Simulink.TsArray. This only occurs when my model uses a Simulink Bus Object for the first root-level inport block.
Workaround: As a workaround, please perform the following steps:1. Quit MATLAB??.2. Navigate to MATLABROOT\toolbox\systemtest\templates\elements\simulink. MATLABROOT is the MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the  MATLABROOT function at the MATLAB command line.3. For backup purposes, rename SIMULINKMAIN.M to SIMULINKMAIN.M.OLD.4. Download the attached file attachment_486633_20080924.zip to a temporary directory.5. Extract the file SIMULINKMAIN.M from the zip file downloaded in step 4 and copy SIMULINKMAIN.M to the directory in step 2.6. Navigate to MATLABROOT\toolbox\systemtest\systemtest\private.7. Extract the file SIMULINKGETSIGNALENDTIME.P from the zip file downloaded in step 4 and copy SIMULINKGETSIGNALENDTIME.P to the directory in step 6.8. Start MATLAB.9. Issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:>> rehash toolboxcache

Summary: The matlabFunction method errors when the input is a constant
Description: The matlabFunction  method of the sym  class errors when the input is a constant sym  object: matlabFunction(sym(1)) ??? Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals. Error in ==> sym.matlabFunction>format at 133varnames(end) = []; Error in ==> sym.matlabFunction at 86varnames = format(v);

Summary: Figures created from Plot General element that use legends sometimes do not properly refresh.
Description: When using a Plot General Test Element with legend turned on, the created figure window does not correctly refresh itself every time you run the test. The figure may look like nothing changed. The figure always appears correct in the generated report. If you minimize/maximize or resize the desktop window, you can see the figure properly refresh.

Summary: PIL errors when MEX is configured to use the LCC compiler and your current working directory is a mapped network drive.
Description: Model block PIL and the PIL block throw the following error when MEX is configured to use the LCC compiler and your current working directory is a mapped network drive: C:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2008B~3\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Rename of 'H:\DOCUME~1\MATLAB\rtwdemo_pil_component_psf.mexw32' failed: No such file or directory This problem is caused by a MEX command bug. See bug report ID 486734 for further information.
Workaround: You can use one of several workarounds for this issue:See the workaround in bug report ID 486734.Change the current working directory so that it is not a mapped network drive.Change the MEX compiler to something other than LCC.

Summary: The MuPAD notebook interface crashes when deleting text annotations like titles and labels in graphics.
Description: To reproduce the crash create a plot with some text annotation. For example Execute the command plot(x^2)  Open the Graphics notebook, and under Canvas-> Annotation-> Header enter a header name. Delete the header name and press Enter.  ?
Workaround: Instead of deleting the text annotation replace the text with a single space character.

Summary: When using the iar dialect, PolySpace crashes with a message saying that snoot.x86-mingw32.exe has encountered some problems
Description: When you start a verification of C code, an error message indicates that snoot.x86-mingw32.exe has crashed. In the PolySpace LOG file, the message raises a signal 5:Received signal 5Exception raised in binary_driverexception sysErr(OS.SysErr(name="Function not implemented: kill",syserror=nosys)) raised.   This happens in the following example when trying to verify C code using iar dialect: typedef unsigned int UINT;typedef struct{  unsigned int nb0:1;} BITS;typedef struct{  UINT nbit0 : 1;  } TYPE_B;typedef union{  unsigned char mbyte;  TYPE_B mybit;} TYPE_P;extern union { unsigned char REG; BITS REG_bit; } @ 0xAAAA;TYPE_P* global1 = (TYPE_P *)&REG;int main(void){}  In this example, the problem is due to the global variable global1  , which points on an anonymous union field REG  .

Summary: "Print to Figure" option in Pixel Region tool fails to print pixel values and grid lines
Description: The "Print to Figure" option in the Pixel Region tool should generate an exact copy of the current view, including image data, grid lines, and pixel value text. Instead, the resulting figure contains only image data.

Summary: The Video and Image Processing Blockset? Mplay function causes an error when you switch sources with the Pixel Region tool open.
Description: MPlay is left in a bad state if it is attached to a Simulink line with the Pixel Region Tool left open. This only happens when MPlay was previously reading an AVI file.
Workaround: There are no workarounds

Summary: Changing block choice of a configurable subsystem with a signal viewer causes MATLAB to terminate execution
Description: Changing the block choice of a configurable subsystem (with a viewer attached) and subsequently simulating the model causes a segmentation violation and the termination of MATLAB execution.
Workaround: No workaround is available for R2007a, R2007b, R2008a or R2008b.

Summary: Simulink might crash when simulating models with specific Sum block configurations
Description: Sum blocks with the following settings can cause Simulink to crash during simulation: Accumulator type must be a built-in integer, explicitly specified using the fixdt method, such as fixdt(1,32,0). Only built-in integers are involved. Overflow mode is set to "wrap". First port must be '+', or single-port '+'.
Workaround: Use alternative methods to specify the accumulator type. Examples:Instead of fixdt(1,32,0), use int32.Use inherited data types, such as "via internal rule" or "same as first input".

Summary: There is a bug in MATLAB code that prevents a deployment application from finding package functions.
Description: Because of an error in MATLAB code, deployment applications that uses multiple components sometimes would fail to find package functions for its second component. The error message looks like the following: The file 'C:\Temp\...\mcr_cache_v79\libcallUdd1_B9D4F979FF5CADB8122734919C3D8173\toolbox\control\control\+ltipack\utDelayStruct.m' is not in the application's expanded CTF archive at 'C:\Temp\...\mcr_cache_v79\libcallUdd2_FAC16A79EC2E7B56979E96890FF7F601'.  This is typically caused by calls to ADDPATH in startup.m  or matlabrc.m  files. Please see the MATLAB Compiler documentation and use the isdeployed  function to ensure ADDPATH commands are not executed by deployed applications.
Workaround: No workaround is currently available.

Summary: mcc complains about nonexistent ../toolbox/compiler/patch directory
Description: mcc  displays the following warning about a nonexistent directory twice: Warning: Name is nonexistent or not a directory: <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/compiler/patch. Warning: Name is nonexistent or not a directory: <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/compiler/patch. Processing include files...  where MATLABROOT  is the MATLAB installation root directory. The capability to have a patch directory was introduced in MATLAB Compiler 4.9 (R2008b). MATLAB files or MEX-files placed in the patch directory will eclipse (take precedence over) files or functions with the same name in any other directory on MATLAB Compiler's path, except the current working directory. If the directory is not present, mcc  issues the above warning messages. This condition has no other adverse effect on the compilation.
Workaround: Create an empty but readable ../toolbox/compiler/patch directory using your operating systems's mkdir utility by entering the following commands:cd <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/compilermkdir patchThe directory must be readable for all users of mcc.

Summary: The C28335 eZdsp Stand alone code using Flash Memory block in the Target Preferences Library has a bad link. The C28335 eZdsp block in the same library is missing definitions for the external memory banks.
Description: When you open the C2000 Target Preferences Library (c2000tgtpreflib), the icon for the C28335 eZdsp Stand alone code using Flash Memory  block has a bad link. You will not be able to use the block in a model. The C28335 eZdsp  Target Preferences block from the same library is missing definitions for the external memory sections ZONE7P and ZONE7D. Therefore, these sections are absent from the block's Memory bank list. You will not be able to use those external memory sections for your generated code.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_485854.zip. To install the patch, follow these steps:1. Start MATLAB.2. Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_485854.zip', 'install');3. Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the patch installation.

Summary: datevec does not consistently round off the seconds field.
Description: datevec does not consistently round off the seconds  field. For example, if you are using the etime  function to measure the elapsed time between two datevecs then, depending on the hour of the day, you get different results. format long g>> start1=datevec('8/14/2008 4:00:00 PM');>> end1=datevec('8/14/2008 4:00:20 PM');>> etime(end1,start1) ans = 20 >> start2=datevec('8/14/2008 5:00:00 PM');>> end2=datevec('8/14/2008 5:00:20 PM');>> etime(end2,start2) ans = 19.9999923706055 Since we are dealing with numbers that are approximately 730,000 (days) * 86400 (secs/day), the best case accuracy we can get is about 730000*86400*eps = 1.4e-5. It is thus not possible to remove all round off inconsistencies.
Workaround: None.

Summary: Legends do not print from the Scope Viewers
Description: Printing Scope Viewer screens that have legends turned on results in hard copies with no legends.

Summary: NEWELM example in documentation fails.
Description: The example in documentation for NEWELM fails: This code generates and error of "too many arguments" for a call to CALCGX from TRAIN: P = round(rand(1,20));T = [0 (P(1:end-1)+P(2:end) == 2)]Pseq = con2seq(P);Tseq = con2seq(T);net = newelm(P,T,10);net = train(net,Pseq,Tseq);  ?
Workaround: The workaround is to fix a bad network setting directly before the call to train:net.gradientFcn = 'calcgbtt';net = train(net,Pseq,Tseq);?

Summary: PolySpace software incorrectly reports a compilation error when logical operations are performed on variables of a modular subtype with constrained range?
Description: If a modular subtype is implicitly defined (for example, through a range constrain), logical operations on variables of this subtype are incorrectly reported as invalid. The following code example illustrates this behavior: package Test is type Mod_T is mod (2 ** 32); type Table_T is array (Mod_T range <>) of Integer; Table : Table_T (0 .. 15) := (others => 0); V_Index : Mod_T := 0; V_Result : Integer;end Test; package body Test isbegin V_Result := Table(V_Index); V_Result := Table(not V_Index); V_Result := Table(V_Index and 16#0000_000F#); V_Result := Table(V_Index or 16#0000_000F#); V_Result := Table(V_Index xor 16#0000_000F#);end Test; The errors given are: -> Verifier found an error in file.ada:15:22: invalid operand type for operator "Not"-> Verifier found an error in file.ada:16:30: invalid context for logical operation-> Verifier found an error in file.ada:17:30: invalid context for logical operation-> Verifier found an error in file.ada:18:30: invalid context for logical operation However, this code should compile without errors.
Workaround: You can workaround this issue by using a temporary variable that holds the result of the logical operation, as in the following example:Tmp : Mod_T;...Tmp := not V_Index; V_Result := Table(Tmp);

Summary: Models with multiple FIR Interpolation blocks can generate bad code
Description: When a model contains two or more FIR Interpolation blocks and the inputs to these blocks have different fixed-point data types, the generated code is incorrect and can cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: Use one or more of the attached files to work around this problem in R2008a or R2008b. There is a file for each platform supported by MATLAB in each of the affected releases. The platform is indicated by the first file extension, and the release is indicated by the second file extension. Download the correct file for your platform and release into the directorymatlabroot/toolbox/dspblks/dspmexwhere matlabroot is the path returned when you type matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.If you are running MATLAB on multiple platforms or are not sure which file to download, download all the files with the extension for your release into the same directory.After downloading the files, change the file names by removing the second file extension. You will first need to remove the existing file or files with the same name. We recommend that you save a copy of your original files before replacing them.

Summary: Building the PIL block fails for subsystems that use multiple model referencing
Description: For a subsystem that refers to other models, the PIL block build process can fail when you try to build a PIL block from the top subsystem. When you right-click the top subsystem to build the PIL block, the build fails while compiling the top executable in Green Hills? MULTI?. MULTI reports a linker error and the build stops. Some library files that PIL requires for the referenced models do not show up in the PIL project for the top subsystem, causing the failure.
Workaround: Follow these steps to download and apply a patch to your software. Apply this patch only to V1.1 (R2008b).Download the attached files patch_485425.zip and patch_installer.m to a temporary directory.Start MATLAB?.At the command prompt, change to the temporary directory where you saved the downloaded files:cd ('driveletter:\path to temporary directory')Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch.patch_installer('patch_485425.zip','install')The patch updates your software to fix the PIL build process.

Summary: Specifying the scale values of a SOS DFILT object as an FI object sometimes results in an error.
Description: Specifying the scale values of a SOS DFILT object as an FI object results in an error when using a fractional format (i.e., fractionlength = wordlength -1  for signed or fractionlength = wordlength  for unsigned). For example: >> g = [.125; .5];H=dfilt.df2sos([1 0 0 1 0 0],g);H.ScaleValues = fi(H.ScaleValues,1,18,17);??? Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.Error in ==> dfilt.filterquantizer.svnoteq2one at 17 iseq2one = iseq2one || isrealmax;Error in ==> dfilt.abstractsos.quantizecoeffs at 24isnoteq2one = svnoteq2one(h.filterquantizer, h.refScaleValues);Error in ==> dfilt.abstractsos.setsv at 18quantizecoeffs(Hd);

Summary: Calling some IRCurve methods with a lower case curve type (e.g. zero) returns an error.
Description: Calling the bootstrap  method of IRDataCurve  or the fitSvensson , fitNelsonSiegel  or fitFunction  methods of IRFunctionCurve  with a lower case curve type (e.g. zero ) causes an error.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, call these methods with an uppercase curve type (e.g.: Zero).

Summary: Simulink may ignore the sample time setting in an Inport dialog box
Description: Simulink may ignore the sample time setting in an Inport dialog box when: The Inport block must be within a subsystem. The input port of the subsystem must not be unconnected. The Inport dialog sample time field contains a valid entry. The Inport is associated with a bus object via the 'Specify properties via a bus object' field in the dialog.  In this situation, Simulink does not honor the sample time setting during simulation, and the input signal can be of any rate.

Summary: COM interface signal objects, created by tagging signals for the Embedded MATLAB block, do not reference the correct signal
Description: xPC Target COM interface signal objects, created from tagging signals from the output the Embedded MATLAB block, do not return the correct reference signal ID.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Stop MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private\xpctagbio.m to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private\xpctagbio_bak.m.Download the attached file, xpctagbio.m, and save it to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private\xpctagbio.mRestart MATLAB.In the MATLAB Command Window, type rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: Text on mask icons rotates with the block when it should remain fixed
Description: Text rendered by the text  command on a mask icon rotates when the block rotates. The text should remain fixed.
Workaround: Add a 'texmode' option with value set to 'on' in the affected text command.

Summary: mcc -e Suppress MS-DOS Command Window should not have quotes around filename. Generates MATLAB syntax error.
Description: When double-quotes are used around the file name when entering the mcc -e  command from MATLAB, the following syntax error results: Error: The input character is not valid in MATLAB statements or expressions.  ? ? ?
Workaround: Instead of using double-quotes in all cases, use the method of quoting your file name based on where you issue your command from, according to standard MATLAB syntax.

Summary: Deployed applications cannot print header text added in Print Preview dialog box.
Description: During interactive printing, deployed applications may display a Print Preview dialog box. Changing a setting or value displayed in the Print Preview dialog box normally affects printed output from a deployed application in the same way it affects printed output in MATLAB. However, since Compiler version 4.8 (R2008a), header text or date information entered in the Print Preview dialog box will not appear in printed output created by deployed applications.

Summary: Plotting a timeseries object having no start date into a held timeseries plot that does can cause a command line error
Description: When a timeseries that lacks an absolute start date is plotted with the timeseries plot  method into a figure with hold  'on' , the following error is produced if the figure being plotted into currently contains a plot of a timeseries that has a non-empty absolute start date. Caught-error breakpoint was hit in timeseries.plot>localEnableResize at line 265. The error was: No public field enabled exists for class handle. The timeseries start date is the StartDate  property of the tsdata.timemetadadata  object stored in the timeseries TimeInfo  property.
Workaround: For MATLAB 7.3 and beyond, find the following line in the file matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@timeseries/plot.m.delete(getappdata(ax,'DateResizeListener'));It will be located between lines 95 and 102 depending on which version of MATLAB you are using.After this line add the following line of MATLAB code:delete(getappdata(ax,'DateResizeListener'));In order to see this change take effect, you will need to clear classes or restart MATLAB.?

Summary: Documentation error on concatenating containers.Map objects.
Description: The R2008b documentation gives conflicting information regarding which type of concatenation is supported for Map containers in MATLAB. Documentation stating that horizontal concatenation of Map objects is supported is in error. MATLAB supports only vertical concatenation of Map objects. The following three examples from the MATLAB documentation also require correction: Programming Fundamentals => Classes (Data Types) => Map Containers => Reading and Writing Using a Key Index => Builting a Map with Concatenation. Example under the third bullet. Code lines 4 through 6. Incorrect:    /% Missing semmicolon following 'tempMap' on first line below.    ticketMap = [tempMap ...    containers.Map({'NZ1452', 'A479GY', '947F4'}, ...    {'Bradley Reid', 'Sarah Latham', 'Susan Spera'})];Correct:    /% Semicolon inserted to concatenate vertically.    ticketMap = [tempMap; ...    containers.Map({'NZ1452', 'A479GY', '947F4'}, ...    {'Bradley Reid', 'Sarah Latham', 'Susan Spera'})];  Same section in documentation. Example under the fourth bullet. Code line 3. Incorrect:    /% Missing semmicolon between m1 and m2.    m = [m1 m2];Correct:    /% Semicolon inserted to concatenate vertically    m = [m1; m2];  Function Reference page for containers.Map. Example 5 ˇŞ Adding More Keys and Values Later. Last line of code in the example. Incorrect:    /% Missing semmicolon between m1 and m2.    US_Capitals = [US_Capitals newMap];Correct:   /% Semicolon inserted to concatenate vertically.   US_Capitals = [US_Capitals; newMap];
Workaround: The documentation will be fixed in the next release of MATLAB. Please keep in mind that only vertical concatenation of Map objects is supported.

Summary: Attempting to open the Simulink Library Browser will cause MATLAB to hang if "Matrox VFW Software Codecs" are installed on your system.
Description: Signal Processing Blockset and Video and Image Processing Blockset are incompatible with "Matrox VFW Software Codecs" in versions of MATLAB prior to R2009a. This incompatibility is due to the enumeration of codecs which is performed by the To Multimedia File block. In order to determine if you have "Matrox VFW Software Codecs" installed on your system, follow these steps:1. Open the following application:Start -> Settings -> Control Panel -> Administrative Tools -> Computer Management2. Select SystemTools -> DeviceManager3. Select Sound, Video and game controllers4. Double click on Video Codecs5. Select the "properties" tab6. Verify "Matrox VFW Software Codecs" is listed ?
Workaround: ?In order to work around this issue in versions of MATLAB prior to R2009a, you should uninstall these codecs. If this is not possible, you may disable the enumeration of compression codecs by downloading the MATLAB file in the attached zip file and installing it as:$matlabroot\toolbox\dspblks\dspmasks\dspwin32getcompressornames.m

Summary: A stack trace occurs when you call SET or GET on a device object
Description: When you call SET or GET with empty constraints on a device object, it results in a stack trace similar to the one below: >> d = icdevice('tektronix_tds210',serial('com3'));>> connect(d)>> groupObj = get(d,'Acquisition');>> set(groupObj) ??? Java exception occurred:java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: 0 atcom.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.device.drivers.xml.property.PropertyDefinition.getPropInfoType(PropertyDefinition.java:1101)  atcom.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.device.drivers.xml.property.PropertyDefinition.configurePropInfo(PropertyDefinition.java:906)  at com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.device.drivers.xml.XMLDriver.getPropertyInformation(XMLDriver.java:342) at com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.device.DeviceChild.getInstrumentPropertyInformation(DeviceChild.java:430) at com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.device.DeviceChild.getPropertyInfo(DeviceChild.java:474) at com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.device.util.Displays.setDisplay(Displays.java:80) at com.mathworks.toolbox.instrument.device.DeviceChild.setDisplay(DeviceChild.java:329)?  Error in ==> icgroup.set>localCreateSetDisplay at 172fprintf(char(setDisplay(igetfield(obj, 'jobject'))));  Error in ==> icgroup.set at 79  localCreateSetDisplay(obj);  ?

Summary: Stateflow debugger sometimes throws spurious "index out of range" errors for very large arrays
Description: The Stateflow debugger may generate spurious "index out of range" errors when simulating a model that meets the following conditions: Specifies an embedded target (ert.tlc  or a variant) as the code generation target Specifies integer size as 16 bits on the target platform in the Hardware Implementation section of the Configuration Parameters dialog box Contains arrays with more than 2^16 elements
Workaround: Turn off debugging during simulation or increase integer size to 32 bits during simulation. For example, in the Hardware Implementation parameters, temporarily set Device vendor to Generic and Device type to Unspecified (assume 32-bit generic) during simulation.

Summary: When External mode is configured, Stateflow chart simulation code might not compile.
Description: When the Real-Time Workshop data-exchange interface is External mode , simulation target code generated for Stateflow charts might not compile due to long strings that few compilers can handle. The length of the string is proportional to the number of states and local data in a chart.
Workaround: Choose one of these methods:If you do not need External mode, change your selection in the Configuration Parameters dialog box: Real-Time Workshop > Interface > Data exchange > Interface.You can divide a big chart, which contains many states and local data, into several smaller charts.

Summary: Build process fails when generating code for ERT-based S-function
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process fails when generating code to implement an ERT S-function interface to a model or subsystem when the model or subsystem references nonscalar parameters with a ImportedExternPointer  storage class. The compilation of the source file implementing the model's S-function wrapper fails, and an error similar to the following is reported in the MATLAB Command Window: matlabroot \BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Compile of 'subsystem_name _sf.c' failed. NMAKE : fatal error U1077: 'matlabroot \bin\mex' : return code '0x9' Stop. working_directory \ subsystem_name _ert_rtw>echo The make command returned an error of 2 The make command returned an error of 2  working_directory \ subsystem_name _ert_rtw>An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make 'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: ' subsystem_name ' aborted due to an error.??? Subsystem build failed

Summary: digitrevorder errors with some valid inputs
Description: digitrevorder produces an error in some cases when you input an integer power, such as  digitrevorder(1:8^3,8);
Workaround: To work around this issue, copy the attachment file to your $ROOT\toolbox\signal\signal directory, overwriting the current digitrevorder.m file.

Summary: A problem with DRS permanent mode may lead to incorrect results.
Description: This bug occurs because permanent mode does not always set the variable as random, within the specified range. For example, consider the following code with rnd in permanent mode between 0 and 1. int rnd;  void main(void) {   int x,y = 1;   if (rnd) y = x; // red NIV   if (rnd) x = y; // pb : grey code here}   Since rnd could be 0 or 1, both branches are reachable. But the second if branch is actually gray.
Workaround: Set the variable (specified in the DRS) as volatile. The range will then be the one specified in the DRS, but the variable will be fully random.

Summary: The run(VDSP_Obj, 'runtohalt') method times out during processor-in-the-loop co-simulation.
Description: Your model uses a Processor-in-the-loop (PIL) block, it generates a Simulink error immediately after you start the co-simulation. The error states that RunToHalt has timed out. This problem affects PIL co-simulation for BlackFin?, SHARC?, and TigerSHARC? processor families. ??? Error using ==> adivdsp.runRunToHalt: Timed out waiting for VDSP to confirm completion. The error is caused by the run method, which has a low default timeout value. The run method does not honor the model-wide timeout setting for the Link for Analog Devices VisualDSP++?.
Workaround: Download the attached patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_483968.zip.Start MATLAB? and set the Current directory to the location of the downloaded files.Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch: patch_installer('patch_483968.zip', 'install')To complete the installation, follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line.The patch modifies the following files in your MATLAB installation:* <matlabroot>\toolbox\vdsplink\vdsplink\+vdsplink\+autointerface\@adivdsp\run.m

Summary: Korean and Chinese characters do not display correctly
Description: When MATLAB is running on English Windows systems configured with a different language (locale setting), such as Korean, and when Tahoma is the font MATLAB uses, characters in that language do not show up correctly. This bug does not affect retail Korean Windows systems. Note that in R2008b, by default, the Current Directory browser, Workspace browser, Variable Editor, and other desktop tools use the Microsoft Windows system font, and on English Windows systems, the system font is usually Tahoma.
Workaround: Specify a different font for MATLAB:Selecting File > Preferences > Fonts.Clear the Use system font check box.Select Dialog from the font drop down list.Click OK.

Summary: In Financial Toolbox?, bndspread errors if any dates in SpotInfo equal the Settle date
Description: If the first column of the SpotInfo  input argument to bndspread  contains a date that is equal to the Settle  date, bndspread  returns an error.
Workaround: Attached is the fix for the problem - note that this fix will only work in versions including and after 2008A.To workaround this issue, replace the version of bndspread.m function in your current installation with the updated version attached.Exit MATLAB?.Make a backup copy of the following file.matlabroot\toolbox\finance\finance\bndspread.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, bndspread.m.OLD.3. Download the attached file and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.4. Restart MATLAB.5. Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The last item on the rmi function reference page syntax list is wrong.
Description: The last syntax line reads rmi('objCopy', object) It should read rmi('copyObj', object)
Workaround: Use the command rmi('copyObj', object) instead of the command rmi('objCopy', object)

Summary: Using END as an array subscript twice on same line at the command prompt can cause MATLAB to crash.
Description: In rare cases, running MATLAB code at the command prompt that uses the end  keyword as an index twice on the same line can cause MATLAB to crash. For example,   c{1} = '"01:01:01.001"'   str2num(c{1}(2:end-1)) + str2num(c{1}(2:end-1))
Workaround: The following command disables use of the MATLAB Accelerator for commands entered at the command prompt. This applies to the current MATLAB session only. Note that although using this command prevents the crash, it can also slow down some commands that you run from the MATLAB command prompt.>> feature jitallow prompt off

Summary: Spurious error in complicated assignments.
Description: Multiple assignments to variables that use various indexing styles on the left-hand side of an expression sometimes generate a misleading error. An example of such an assignment is shown here:   [a{1}, b.one, c.two, d] = ...; The incorrect error that can be generated by this particular statement is, "Illegal right hand side in assignment. Too many elements."
Workaround: Simplify the left-hand side of the statement by using temporary variables.  The above assignment can be written as   [T1, T2, T3, d] = ...;   a{1} = T1;   b.one = T2;   c.two = T3;

Summary: Error when using the PolySpace Excel macro with Excel 2007
Description: When using the PolySpace Excel macro, after you choose the RTE_view.txt fle, Excel gives the following error: Run-time error '-2147024809 (80070057)': This object has no title. When in debug mode, Visual Basic also highlights a line in the function createOrangeRepartitionGraph.
Workaround: A new version of the Excel macro is attached to this EBR.Note that following workaround is only available for release R2008b.To workaround this issue under Linux: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:$POLYSPACE_C/lib/PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Linux or Solaris machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_482793_client_R2008b_linux_2008-12-06.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (/usr/local/Polyspace by default under Linux) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacementsTo workaround this issue under Windows: 1. Create a backup copy of the following file:POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\lib\PolySpace_Macros.xls for a Windows machine 2. Download the attached file attachment_482793_client_R2008b_win_2008-12-06.zip. 3. Browse to the Polyspace installation folder (C:\Polyspace by default under Windows) 4. Unzip the file in this installation folder and confirm the replacementsAll PolySpace verifications launched after you copy this file will automatically include the new PolySpace_Macros.xls file in the PolySpace-Doc folder of each results directory.To generate reports for existing PolySpace results, copy the PolySpace_Macros.xls file to the 'PolySpace-Doc' folder of each existing results directory.Note: If you are using Excel 2007, you must bypass the security warning displayed under the Excel toolbar. To bypass the security warning:Click Options next to the message.Select Enable this content in the dialog window.Click Ok.

Summary: Generating embedded HTML code from WebFigures only works on select Web servers.
Description: When using the MATLAB Builder JA WebFigures feature, you embed the WebFigure into a Web page either by using the JSP tag or by using the old or new WebFigure method. If you use the deprecated method: WebFigures webFigures = new WebFigures("WebFigures",getServletContext());String embedString = webFigures.getHtmlEmbedString(wb,"Vararg_Figure", "session", "", "", "");  Or the newer version: WebFigureHtmlGenerator webFigures = new WebFigureHtmlGenerator("WebFigures", getServletContext());String embedString = webFigures.getFigureEmbedString(wb,"Vararg_Figure", "session", "", "", "");  Or the JSP tag: <wf:web-figure name="UserPlot" scope="session" root="WebFigures"  width="100%" height="100%"/>  You may receive this error: java.io.IOException: The filename, directory name, or volume label syntax is incorrect  at java.io.WinNTFileSystem.canonicalize0(Native Method)  at java.io.Win32FileSystem.canonicalize(Unknown Source)  at java.io.File.getCanonicalPath(Unknown Source)  This error occurs because (in each of these methods) the getFigureEmbedString  is being called, executing code similar to this:URL path = getServletContext().getResource("/");  In Tomcat, this returns a JNDI URL referencing the root application of the web server, such as http://localhost:8080/MyApp/ . When using other Web servers, a reference (to a file which cannot be used to redirect) may be returned. The Jetty Web server is known to be affected by this issue. ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, do not use the JSP tag library. In addition, provide a fully qualified URL to indicate where WebFigureService is installed in your Web application. For example:WebFigureHtmlGenerator webFigures =  new WebFigureHtmlGenerator("http://localhost:8080/MyApp/WebFigures/");. Note: this workaround is only available in MATLAB R2008b or newer.

Summary: The fprintf function doesn't work properly after an fgetl reaches the end of a file and then ferror is called.
Description: Internally, the error on the file is not being reset properly.
Workaround: Close the file and then reopen it. You can then write to it again.

Summary: Printing a Stateflow chart to a color printer produces a printout with no colors for transitions and text
Description: Stateflow objects, e.g.,  transitions, text, etc., do not have colors in a printout of a Stateflow chart produced by a color printer.

Summary: Missing plot symbol for disttool with a discrete distribution
Description: The disttool  function can plot a cdf  (cumulative distribution function) or a pdf  (probability density function) for a distribution. For a discrete distribution, such as the Poisson distribution, the pdf  is plotted as a series of markers indicating the probability for the x  values 0, 1, 2, etc. Under some conditions, a marker at the beginning or end of the axis may be clipped. For example, with the Poisson distribution, the marker for the probability at x=0  may not appear.
Workaround: You can adjust the x-axis limits using tools in the figure window. From the Tools menu, select the Pan tool. Click in the plot area and use the "hand" cursor to drag the plot slightly to the right so that the point at x=0 moves into the interior of the plot.When you are done adjusting the axis limits, return to the Tools menu and de-select the Pan tool so that you can continue using the mouse to click on an x value and read the corresponding y value.

Summary: Opening a subsystem with the Simulink model window reuse preference enabled can crash MATLAB
Description: Opening a subsystem with the Simulink model window reuse preference enabled can crash MATLAB. This occurs when the diagram containing the subsystem also contains a From block.
Workaround: Avoid using the window reuse preference for opening subsystems if you encounter this problem.

Summary: Printing a subsystem or chart brings the model forward
Description: Printing a subsystem or a Stateflow chart brings the model forward after printing instead of the chart or the subsystem that was printed.

Summary: Using an extrinsic function as the condition of a control-flow statement causes errors in Embedded MATLAB
Description: If you use the result of an extrinsic function as the condition for an if  or while  statement in an Embedded MATLAB function, you get an assertion.
Workaround: Assign the result of the extrinsic function to a temporary variable that matches the type returned by the extrinsic function. Then, use that variable as the condition for the if or while statement. For example, the following code causes an error:eml.extrinsic('externalTestFcn')if externalTestFcn()  y = 1;else  y = 2;endTo eliminate the error, assign the output of externalTestFcn to a temporary variable testIt. Then use testIt as the condition in the if statement, as follows:eml.extrinsic('externalTestFcn')testIt = false; % should match the type returned by the extrinsic functiontestIt = externalTestFcn();if testIt  y = 1;else  y = 2;end??

Summary: Simulation And Code Generation Mismatch: Code generation and simulation results do not match for Stateflow charts with integer overflow
Description: For Stateflow charts, code generation and simulation results do not match if these conditions are true: Chart actions contain arithmetic operations between signed and unsigned integer values. Integer overflow occurs.
Workaround: Verify that actions in your chart do not trigger integer overflows.For example, suppose that you have an expression a = b + c, where a, b, and c have uint8 data types. If b and cequal 200, overflow occurs because a cannot hold any integer value greater than 255.

Summary: Using MATLAB? Report Generator? to generate a report to a directory named with non-ascii characters fails
Description: Generating HTML output to a directory with non-ascii characters displays the following error: Document conversion failed . Generating DOC/PDF output to a directory with non-ascii characters displays the following error: Converting RTF to DOC Conversion from RTF to DOC failed Java exception occurred: java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.toolbox.rptgencore.output.OutputFormat.getExtension(OutputFormat.java:132)
Workaround: To workaround this issue, generate reports to a directory named with ascii characters.

Summary: If two blocks are pointing to the same lookup table, a compilation error occurs.
Description: If two blocks in a model point to the same function/lookup table, PolySpace Model Link TL generates two stubs with same name.This leads to a compilation error on an internal file: Verifying sources ...Verifying __pststubs_tl.c__pststubs_tl.c:133: redefinition of `Block_Look_Up_Table' previously defined at __pststubs_tl.c:114  ?
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your Polyspace installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your Polyspace installation. By default this is in c:\PolySpace or in /usr/local/PolySpace on Linux systems. This folder is referred to as <POLYSPACE> in subsequent steps. PolySpace_Common and PolySpaceFor<lang>_R20xx are the two principal sub-folders of <POLYSPACE>.Exit any Polyspace Graphical IntferfaceDownload the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell:unzip -l <POLYSPACE>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <POLYSPACE> folder.Linux Systems: Type the following in a shell: cd <POLYSPACE>unzip <.zip file>Check that binaries have appropriate ˇ®read & executeˇŻ permissions.Delete the .zip file in the <POLYSPACE> folder.

Summary: D/A output for the 16 bit D/A version of the Diamond MM-32DX does not work
Description: Diamond MM-32DX is a new version of the Diamond MM-32, from Diamond Systems. You can purchase this board with either a 12 bit D/A or a 16 bit D/A. If you purchase the 16 bit D/A version, you must download the attached modified driver to work with xPC Target.  You do not need to download a modified driver if you purchase the 12 bit version of the board.
Workaround: As a workaround,Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file dadiamondmmx.c and save it tomatlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocksChange directory to the xpcblocks directoryRun mex on dadiamondmmx.c: > mex dadiamondmmx.cRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.

Summary: PolySpace JSF C++ Checker does not work on the demo file Demo_Cpp_Long
Description: If you launch a verification of %POLYSPACE_CPP%\Verifier\Examples\Demo_Cpp_Long  using the option -jsf-coding-rules all-rules  (which enables the JSF++ rules checker), no errors or warnings are reported in the log file. The problem occurs because of an issue with the Windows architecture.
Workaround: To use the JSF++ checker on Demo_Cpp_Long, move the complete directory %POLYSPACE_CPP%\Verifier\Examples\Demo_Cpp_Long to any location outside of %POLYSPACE_CPP%\Verifier.

Summary: There is a limit of 4096 characters on the length of expression that initializes a property in the new object system.
Description: Initalizing a property in a class with a very large initialization expression (over 4000 characters) can cause syntax error messages or part of the initializer to be truncated.
Workaround: There are two workarounds. The property can be initialized in the constructor for the class. Or a local function (subfunction) can be written that returns the value of the initializer, and this function can be used to do the initialization of the property.

Summary: sfprint does not work with Stateflow charts containing Simulink functions
Description: You cannot use sfprint  to print a Stateflow chart that contains Simulink functions if you pass the Simulink block handle as the first argument to sfprint . This bug occurs only in Stateflow 7.2 (R2008b) and produces the following error message: Warning, nothing to print --no Stateflow Charts resolved from input args to sfprint()!
Workaround: There are several possible workarounds:Print from the Stateflow Editor File menu rather than using sfprint.Pass the chart ID to sfprint rather than the Simulink block handle. Use the Stateflow UDD API to obtain the Stateflow chart ID.Alternatively, you can apply a MATLAB file patch if you have write permission to the MATLAB installation area. Please do the following:On the MATLAB prompt, type which private/get_charts_of.mCreate a backup of the file printed by the above commandDownload the attached file get_charts_of.m and place it at the location pointed to by the command in step 1.

Summary: The Automatic Orange Tester (AOT) cannot generate instrumented code when arrays use const pointers (for 16-bit targets)
Description: The Automatic Orange Tester (AOT) cannot generate instrumented code when arrays use const pointers. For example, the following code leads to the problem: char * const tab[] = {"1", "2"}; int main(int argc, char ** argv) { tab[0] = 0;  return 0;}  The software displays the following error message: "Error: field 1 of `struct _instrum_g20' overlaps previous field"

Summary: A DLL (shared library or .NET assembly) with the same name as a MATLAB file gets included in the CTF while using the mcc command in MATLAB 7.6 (R2008a) resulting in an "Invalid MEX-file" error at run-time.
Description: This problem occurs when a MATLAB file and a non-mex DLL (shared library or .NET assembly) with the same name are present in the same directory. If mcc  is invoked to compile this MATLAB file into a deployment target (e.g.: a standalone executable), the generated application fails at run-time.  The error message thrown varies depending upon the type of DLL, but it resembles Invalid MEX-file  or MEX-file entry point is missing .  This occurs because mcc  assumes the DLL to be a MEX file [DLL is a secondary extension for MEX-files on Windows; the primary extension is platform-specific (e.g.: MEXW32 on 32-bit Windows)] and when a MEX and a MATLAB file with the same name are present in the same directory, the MEX file gets precedence over the MATLAB file and it gets written to the CTF, resulting in the run-time failure.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this problem. Users should copy the MATLAB file to a different directory before compiling it.

Summary: Compare To Constant and Compare To Zero blocks: error evaluating MaskCallback function
Description: When you double-click Compare To Constant or Compare To Zero blocks in a model, this error appears: "Error evaluating MaskCallback".  This bug occurs when a model attaches a configuration set via a configuration reference and marks it active. The MaskCallback function does not handle the active configuration reference correctly. This bug does not occur when you attach the actual configuration set to the model.
Workaround: Attach the actual configuration set to the model without using a configuration reference.

Summary: PIL S-function error caused by incorrect dimensions for a frame-based signal
Description: If a Signal Processing Blockset Frame Conversion block operating in "Inherit output sampling mode from <Ref> input port" mode changes the sampling mode (i.e. sample-based or frame-based) of a root outport signal with non-auto storage class, then PIL throws the following error: ??? S-function 'alg_psf' in 'untitled/alg' sets number of dimensions to be one for its output port 3 even though that port is frame-based. Note that if a port is frame-based, then the number of dimensions should be at least 2.
Workaround: Replace matlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/+rtw/+connectivity/@CodeInfoTypeUtils/CodeInfoTypeUtils.m with attached version.

Summary: Link error when generating code for a DM642 model.
Description: The CCS window reports the following a link error when you generate code for DM642EVM models: >> warning: Detected a near (.bss section relative) data reference to the symbol _EVMDM642_I2C_hI2C defined in section .far. The reference occurs in H:\\Documents\\MATLAB\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP_ccslink\\CustomMW\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP.obj, section .text, SPC offset 0000116c. Either make the symbol near data by placing it in the .bss section, or make the references to the symbol far. For C/C++ code use 'far' or 'near' modifiers on the type definition of the symbol or compile with the --mem_model:data switch.>> error: relocation overflow occurred at address 0x0000116c in section '.text' of input file 'H:\\Documents\\MATLAB\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP_ccslink\\CustomMW\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP.obj'. The 18-bit relocated address 0x2add0 is too large to encode in the 15-bit field. You may need to add a mask to the assembly instruction or use other target specific assembly features if you really only need the lowest 15 bits of this symbol. Please see the section on Relocation in the Assembly User's Guide.>> error: relocation overflow occurred at address 0x00001230 in section '.text' of input file 'H:\\Documents\\MATLAB\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP_ccslink\\CustomMW\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP.obj'. The 18-bit relocated address 0x2add0 is too large to encode in the 15-bit field. You may need to add a mask to the assembly instruction or use other target specific assembly features if you really only need the lowest 15 bits of this symbol. Please see the section on Relocation in the Assembly User's Guide.>> error: relocation overflow occurred at address 0x00001368 in section '.text' of input file 'H:\\Documents\\MATLAB\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP_ccslink\\CustomMW\\idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP.obj'. The 18-bit relocated address 0x2add0 is too large to encode in the 15-bit field. You may need to add a mask to the assembly instruction or use other target specific assembly features if you really only need the lowest 15 bits of this symbol. Please see the section on Relocation in the Assembly User's Guide.>> warning: output file 'idm642_two_dac_YCbCr_6_inter_TVP.out' is not executable
Workaround: The link error is located in the evmdm642bsl.lib board support library provided by Spectrum Digital. The link error can be fixed by re-compiling this library with correct memory model settings. To do this:1. Open evmdm642bsl.pjt library project that resides under:..\CCSv3.3\boards\evmdm642\lib\evmdm642bsl2. Set project configuration to Release.3. Select Project->Build Options.4. Set Compiler->Advanced->Memory Models to Far Aggregate.5. Recompile the library.6. After build operation is finished, copy ..\CCSv3.3\boards\evmdm642\lib\evmdm642bsl\Release\evmdm642bsl.lib to ..\CCSv3.3\boards\evmdm642\lib.

Summary: S-Function generation fails for subsystem containing Stateflow charts
Description: If a model contains machine-parented Stateflow events, generating an S-Function for a subsystem containing Stateflow charts can fail when the Use Embedded Coder  check box is cleared on the Generate S-Function dialog box. The generated S-Function C code might not compile.

Summary: Stateflow might generate invalid code when parameter scope data is used in chart functions
Description: When your model is configured to generate reusable code, Stateflow charts might generate uncompilable code when parameter scope data is used in chart functions. To check your model configuration, open the Configuration Parameters dialog box and go to the Real-Time Workshop > Interface  pane. If the Generate reusable code  check box is selected, your model is configured to generate reusable code.
Workaround: Check if the source of the compiler error comes from an uninlined graphical, Embedded MATLAB, or truth table function. For the uninlined function, set Function Inline Option to Inline in the properties dialog box.

Summary: Error when you use softscope with Dependent triggering
Description: When you use the Data Acquisition Toolbox softscope and select Dependent  triggering, you will see this error message: An error occurred while starting the analog object
Workaround: To resolve this issue, replace the privateUDDToMATLAB.m file with the the attached file:Quit MATLAB.Rename "privateUDDToMATLAB.m" in [MATLAB]\toolbox\daq\daq\private directory to "privateUDDToMATLAB_old.m".Download, extract, and copy the attached file (privateUDDToMATLAB.m) to the [MATLAB]\toolbox\daq\daq\private directory.Restart MATLAB. The error should no longer appear. Note: [MATLAB] is the directory where you installed MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect dspdata.psd example in documentation
Description: The example on the dspdata.psd  reference page is incorrect. The power spectrum computed using the FFT is double-sided, but is passed to dspdata.psd  as one-sided, which causes the resulting spectrum plot to show peaks at incorrect values.
Workaround: To fix this example, change this line in the example:  Hpsd = dspdata.psd(Pxx,'Fs',Fs);to this:  Hpsd = dspdata.psd(Pxx,'Fs',Fs,'SpectrumType','doublesided');

Summary: Memory leak when calling C shared libraries with varargin arguments.
Description: A memory leak occurs when calling an M-function that contains a varargin  input argument through the C shared library interface. This results in a gradual increase in memory usage by the application when the function is called repeatedly and can also lead to a gradual performance degredation if the function is called many times in the same process.
Workaround: There are no workarounds.

Summary: Binary conversion of Inf, -Inf and NaN in fprintf and sprintf is broken.
Description: The fprintf  and sprintf  conversions %o , %u , %x , and %X  (corresponding to octal, decimal, lowercase hexadecimal, and uppercase hexadecimal, respectively) support the special subtype modifiers b  and t  (corresponding to double and single, respectively). These conversions, along with their b  and t  modifiers, allow for the underlying bit representation of floating point data to be displayed. Conversions of the NaN , Inf  and -Inf  values, which are represented using reserved floating point values are not working, and instead NaN , Inf  and -Inf  are displayed instead. For example, the following call to sprintf shows the erroneous behavior: >> sprintf('%bX\n',[NaN Inf -Inf])      ans =      NaN      Inf      -Inf The correct behavior should be: >> sprintf('%bX\n',[NaN Inf -Inf])      ans =      FFF8000000000000     7FF0000000000000     FFF0000000000000

Summary: After creating a new MATLAB Builder JA project and clicking the Build button, I receive the following error at the command prompt:Unable to create com.mathworks.toolbox.compiler.OutputWindow, for details see C:\Temp\MATLABDesktopCreateError.
Description: This error occurs everytime the build action is initiated from deploytool . The first few lines of the error log are java.lang.IllegalAccessException: Class com.mathworks.mwswing.desk.DTClient can not access a member of class com.mathworks.toolbox.compiler.OutputWindow with modifiers "private"java.lang.IllegalAccessException: Class com.mathworks.mwswing.desk.DTClient can not access a member of class com.mathworks.toolbox.compiler.OutputWindow with modifiers "private"  at sun.reflect.Reflection.ensureMemberAccess(Unknown Source)
Workaround: Close deploytool and restart MATLAB to resolve the problem. If the problem persists delete the MATLABDesktop.xml file from your preference directory. To do this, execute the following command from MATLAB command prompt:deletefile(fullfile(prefdir,'MATLABDesktop.xml'))If the problem still persists, delete/rename the entire Preferences directory. You can find the location of Preferences directory by executing the command prefdir at the MATLAB command prompt.

Summary: Value for option -path-sensitivity-delta is set twice, both when associated to option -O3 and when a verification is sent in remote mode
Description: Two different values for -path-sensitivity-delta  are listed in the log file, even if you only set one value. This occurs because one value is set for the launching command, and another value is set after the verification is sent to the server in remote mode. In addition, a warning appears in the server log file because of these two different values. For example, if you set -O3 , and then also set -path-sensitivity-delta  to a value of -2  , the following warning appears at the beginning of the server log showing two differents values:WARNING: Options between -prepare-remote & -finalize-remote are not the same:... -prog=Demo_C -polyspace-version=C_R2008a -path-sensitivity-delta=3 -main-generator=true ... -O=-O3... -prog=Demo_C -polyspace-version=C_R2008a -path-sensitivity-delta=8 -main-generator=true ... -O=-O3  Note:  When setting the -O3  option, the -path-sensitivity-delta  value is added to 5 by default. ?

Summary: dftool generates a startup error on Windows 64 machines.
Description: On Windows 64 machines, dftool  will generate an error at startup regarding a missing file, bbfields.mat . dftool  will continue to work for the datasource Yahoo but generate a non fatal error each time Bloomberg is chosen.

Summary: Selecting an object with an empty parameter dialog can crash the Model Explorer
Description: Some objects, for example, EML blocks, use an invalid method for indicating that they do not have a parameter dialog box. Selecting such objects in the Model Explorer's tree view can cause the Model Explorer to crash.

Summary: Using MXGETPROPERTY or MXSETPROPERTY in a MEX-file on some types of objects causes a segmentation violation.
Description: The behavior of MXGETPROPERTY and MXSETPROPERTY in a MEX-file is undefined for objects that are not defined using the CLASSDEF keyword. As a result they can sometimes cause segmentation violation. An example of a stack trace that is caused by this may look something like: Stack Trace: [0] libmx.dll:_mxGetProperty_730(0x1e51d2a0, 0, 0x19e7304c "SL_Integrator1", 0x00d3e530) + 31 bytes [1] libmx.dll:_mxGetProperty_700(0x1e51d2a0, 0, 0x19e7304c "SL_Integrator1", 0x00d3eb64) + 26 bytes [2] rep.mexw32:0x19e713bf(0, 0x00d3eb64, 0, 0x00d3ebc4) [3] libmex.dll:_mexRunMexFile(0, 0x00d3eb64, 0, 0x00d3ebc4) + 129 bytes [4] libmex.dll:private: void __thiscall Mfh_mex::runMexFileWithSignalProtection(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *)(0, 0x00d3eb64, 0, 0x00d3ebc4) + 73 bytes [5] libmex.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall Mfh_mex::dispatch_file(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *)(0, 0x00d3eb64, 0, 0x00d3ebc4) + 220 bytes [6] m_dispatcher.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall Mfh_file::dispatch_fh(int,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag * *)(0, 0x00d3eb64, 0, 0x00d3ebc4) + 193 bytes [7] m_interpreter.dll:__catch$?inDispatchFromStack@@YAHHPBDHH@Z$0(691, 0x03acbeec "rep", 0, 0) + 712 bytes
Workaround: To work around this issue, only use MXGETPROPERTY and MXSETPROPERTY on objects defined using the CLASSDEF keyword.

Summary: TLC function LibNumDiscreteSampleTimes() returns number of synchronous discrete and continuous sample times rather than number of synchronous discrete sample times
Description: Real-Time Workshop documentation describes the Target Language Compiler (TLC) function LibNumDiscreteSampleTimes()  as returning the number of synchronous discrete sample times in the model. Instead, LibNumDiscreteSampleTimes()  returns the number of synchronous discrete and continuous sample times. For example, the function can return a nonzero result in a model that contains no discrete blocks and uses a continuous solver.

Summary: hg.hgjavacomponent error when using Toolbar Editor in GUIDE
Description: When using the GUIDE Toolbar Editor, you might see the following error: ??? No appropriate method, property, or field updateVisibility for class hg.hgjavacomponent. Error in ==> uitools.uitabgroup.schema>onSelChanged at 211 children(i).updateVisibility(); ??? Error while evaluating com.mathworks.hg.peer.UITabGroupPeer$1 StateChangedCallback This can occur if the ShowHiddenHandles  property on the root object is 'on' . You can determine if this is the case by typing the following MATLAB command: get(0,'ShowHiddenHandles')
Workaround: To prevent this error, set the root object's 'ShowHiddenHandles' property to 'off' when using GUIDE:set(0,'ShowHiddenHandles', 'off');

Summary: With a MATLAB pool job, numlabs is set incorrectly on the lab that controls the execution of parallel language constructs such as PARFOR.
Description: When running a MATLAB pool job (createMatlabPoolJob ), the value of numlabs  is incorrectly set on the controlling lab, i.e., the lab that controls the execution of parallel language constructs such as PARFOR.
Workaround: Do not attempt to use any MPI functionality directly from the controlling lab of a MATLAB pool job.

Summary: PolySpace software reports a "Signal 5" compiliation error during verification when using FD_ZERO MACRO
Description: PolySpace verification aborts with the following message in the log file (Windows XP): Verifying sources ...Verifying my_source.cReceived signal 5Exception raised in binary_driverexception SysErr(OS.SysErr(name="Function not implemented : kill", syserror=nosys)) raised.  A standard Windows error message is also shown on read access failure in porky.x86-mingw32.exe . Note : The error message "Received signal 5 ... exception SysErr(OS.SysErr(name="Function ... " is a general message that can occur for many reasons. One of these reasons is because of FD_ZERO MACRO. If you are using FD_MACRO, please see the following workaround.
Workaround: A patch is available in attachment. Please read the file 'install.txt' for setup instructions.

Summary: VSFLEX.vsFlexArrayCtrl hangs when referenced in a MATLAB Builder EX component accessed by Excel.
Description: When using either MATLAB Builder EX or MATLAB Builder NE to build a COM component referencing third party control VSFLEX.vsFlexArrayCtrl , Excel hangs before the control is displayed. This is due to a threading model mismatch between the generated COM component and the FlexArray  control.
Workaround: Changing the threading model of the inproc server to neutral will allow the FlexArray control to run properly. This can be done manually using the regedit GUI or by downloading the attached file and running the following command from a DOS window:regedit /s FlexArray.reg

Summary: Calling libTerminate on a MATLAB C/C++ shared library disables other MATLAB compiled shared libraries in a process
Description: This only happens if the library initialization calls are not followed by calls to mclInhibitShutdown  and library termination calls are not proceeded by calls to mclUninhibitShutdown  (see the Workaround). The reason for this failure is that part of the MCR is terminated prematurely when the call to lib Terminate  is made.
Workaround: Call mclInhibitShutdown immediately after mclInitializeApplication and call mclUninhibitShutdown immediately before mclTerminateApplication.

Summary: feof returns true when you fread to the exact end of the file with a skip byte value greater than 8192.
Description: When you have a file with N bytes, where N > 8192, and you try to read some number of bytes and then skip to the exact end of file location, MATLAB sets the EOF condition to true  if the number of bytes to skip is also > 8192. EOF is only supposed to be set if you have tried to read past the end of the file. For example, the following command reads M bytes and then skips the remaining N-M bytes to get to the end of the file:     fread(fid2, M, 'M*uint8', N-M); If (N-M) > 8192, EOF is set, even though you have not read past the actual end of the file.
Workaround: fread everything, and then only extract the pieces that are needed.

Summary: PolySpace software for C++ crashes with the FFT error: "store_c_to_cpp_sourcepoint"
Description: When trying to verify C-code as part of a C++ verification project, the verification fails with the following message: Stubbing undefined and wrote selected global variables ...* Generating definition and initialization for variable: var1* Generating definition and initialization for variable: var2* Generating definition and initialization for variable: MyClass::var3...----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: store_c_to_cpp_sourcepoint (pst_file#.c , 1234) already doneAn internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information,the computation has been halted.Please complete a Bug Report at The MathWorks Support sitehttps://www.mathworks.com/support.Please remember to attach the log file from this analysis to the report.----------------------------------------------------------------------  This happens when using the ternary operator "?:" in a multi-line construction like the following example: int i = ((a<b) && (a>(c>0)? c:  0x0));
Workaround: To avoid this issue, put the ternary operator "?:" construction on one line.For the above example, this would be:int i = ((a<b) && (a>(c>0)? c: 0x0));

Summary: Unexpected constant type
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on a C++ file, an FFT error occurs at the end of global variable generation and initialization with the following message:"Unexpected constant type"  . The Log shows a message similar to the following: Stubbing undefined and wrote selected global variables ...* Generating definition for constant variable: myclass1::myconstant* Generating definition and initialization for variable: gvar1* Generating definition and initialization for variable: gvar2* Generating definition and initialization for variable: gvar3* Writing variable: myclass2::gvar4----------------------------------------------------------------------@FFT: Unexpected constant typeAn internal consistency problem has been detected.In order to avoid generating incomplete verification information, the computation has been halted.Thanks for filling a Polyspace Technologies Fact Sheet.---------------------------------------------------...  This problem is due to the definition of a static member not redefined in the C++ file. For example: class myclass1 { static const int myconstant = 1; }  without definition outside the class or struct "const  int myclass1::myconstant; ".

Summary: Dragging a populated button group into or out of another container in GUIDE terminates MATLAB prematurely
Description: In GUIDE, if you attempt to drag a button group into another button group or into a panel, MATLAB terminates prematurely as a result of attempting to reset the parent of the buttons inside inside the button group to another container object. The same problem occurs if you place a button group within a panel or button group, add buttons to it, and then attempt to drag the button group outside of the panel or button group.
Workaround: Do not drag a button group into another button group or panel in GUIDE. Do not drag a button group that is in a panel or another button group outside its container.

Summary: The Multipath Rayleigh and Rician Fading Channel blocks are slower in R2007B when compared to R2007A.
Description: The Multipath Rayleigh and Rician Fading Channel blocks are slower in R2007B, R2008A, R2008B when compared to R2007A. In R2007B, the underlying code was updated to support variable Doppler Spectrum shapes per path which caused the decrease in speed. ?

Summary: Signal 11 or Signal 5 error occurs during verification when the source code contains specific constructs
Description: A signal 11  or signal 5  error (depending on the platform) occurs during the compilation phase when a condition statement starts with 0. This small example reproduces the problem: unsigned char dummy;void f(void) { if ((0) && dummy) { // some code assert(0); }}    Example of error mesage under Windows : Generating the Main ...Generating init for variable: dummyGenerating call to function: fDoing code transformations ...  Received signal 5Exception raised in binary_driverexception SysErr(OS.SysErr(name="Function not implemented : kill", syserror=nosys)) raised.  ?
Workaround: There are some ways to eliminate this problem:Change the code and replace:if ((0) && something_dummy)withif (0)Construction like if (1 || something) can also be replace with if (something || 1)Use the option -cfe-extra-flags -no-unr-simplif. To use this option:1. Select PolySpace inner settings > Other options > Others. 2. Enter -cfe-extra-flags -no-unr-simplif.

Summary: lsqr with a function handle errors when maxit argument is empty.
Description: If the first argument of lsqr is a function and the fourth argument is empty, MATLAB? throws an error. For example: A = @(x, opt) [.5 1; 1 .5]*x;b = [0; 1]; x0 = [1; 1];lsqr(A, b, [], [], [], [], x0);
Workaround: Specify the maximum number of iterations (i.e., set the fourth argument explicitly). When this argument is empty, the default behavior for lsqr is to take maxit to be min([m n 20]) where m is the number of rows of b, and n is the number of rows of x0, provided that x0 is in the domain of the linear operator specified by A. In the code above, for examplelsqr(A, b, [], min([size(b,1), size(x0, 1), 20]), [], [], x0)will work as expected.

Summary: Segmentation violation when model has a Custom Joint with only one prismatic or revolute primitive whose names are not P1 or R1, respectively
Description: Updating a SimMechanics model (Ctrl+D ) that has a Custom Joint with only one prismatic or revolute primitive causes a segmentation violation if the primitives do not have the default names P1 or R1, respectively. For example, if the Custom Joint has only one revolute primitive and the primitive is called R2, then updating or running the model causes a segmentation violation.
Workaround: Rename the prismatic or revolute joint primitive to be P1 (prismatic) or R1 (revolute), respectively.

Summary: PolySpace reports a false green when a DRS specifies a range on a complete structure, causing the main generator to not write the variable.
Description: DRS (Data Range Specification) feature works with structure fields, not complete structures. If you use DRS to specify a range on a complete structure, the range is not applied, and a warning message is issued. However, doing this prevents the main generator from writing full range to any of the structure elements. Having initialization values (for example 0 if there is no explicit initialization) instead of full range will lead to false green checks. This problem can occur when you are using the PolySpace Model Link SL product. Any base workspace object with the same name as a bus will cause this situation to happen. If your bus is the set of system inputs, this results in the verification returning all green because zero was used for all of the inputs.

Summary: PolySpace software incorrectly displays a "missing prototype for function" warning in the log file when using the option -function-called-before-main
Description: When verifying C code using the option -function-called-before-main 'myfunction' , the software incorrectly displays the following warning in the log file:'warning: missing prototype for function myfunction ' Note : The verification does not stop and there is no effect on the results. For example: C-STUBS/__polyspace_main.c:67: warning: missing prototype for function 'myfunction'C-STUBS/__polyspace_main.c:67: warning: implicit declaration of 'int myfunction (?)'C-STUBS/__polyspace_main.c:67: Warning: global declaration of 'myfunction' function has incompatible type with its definition  declared function type has incompatible return type with definition  declared 'int' (size 32) type incompatible with defined 'void' (size 0) type  declared function type has unknown args. incompatible with its definition  fct.c:8: other localisation for previous warning  This warning appears on the following example when you use -function-called-before-main 'myfunction' : int glob;void myfunction(void){  glob = 0;}void unused_func(void){  myfunction();}  ?

Summary: Generated MATLAB file sets report "Format" property incorrectly
Description: If you create a report in PDF format with the MATLAB Report Generator, and afterwards generate the MATLAB file equivalent of this same file, the MATLAB file report object does not have the correct output format.
Workaround: To work around this problem, use the following patches: matlab\toolbox\matlab\iofun\@rptgen\@coutline\schema.p matlab\toolbox\matlab\iofun\@rptgen\@coutline\mcodeConstructor.p

Summary: Unexpected CPU overloads might occur when you run a real-time model under polling mode on a target PC
Description: Unexpected CPU overloads might occur when you run a real-time model under polling mode on a target PC.
Workaround: As a workaround, consider running the model under interrupt mode instead of polling mode.
Products: Simulink Real-Time, xPC Target Embedded Option

Summary: Java exception when opening existing GUI for editing in GUIDE
Description: If you attempt to open a GUIDE GUI created using a version of MATLAB prior to R2008a with GUIDE in R2008a, the GUI cannot be opened and you might receive a Java exception similar to the following: java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: -1 at com.mathworks.toolbox.matlab.guide.palette.GObjectProxy.buildComponent(GObjectProxy.java:307) at com.mathworks.toolbox.matlab.guide.palette.MObjectProxy.init(MObjectProxy.java:142) at com.mathworks.toolbox.matlab.guide.palette.GObjectProxy.(GObjectProxy.java:140) at com.mathworks.jmi.NativeMatlab.SendMatlabMessage(Native Method) Error in ==> guide at 132 result = LayoutEditor.openLayoutEditor(filename);
Workaround: The problem can happen if the FIG-file generated by GUIDE was somehow modified outside of GUIDE. For example, a GUI which generates graphics might have been saved, overwriting the original GUIDE-generated FIG-file.If you are running MATLAB R2008a, to work around this issue download the attached file, guidemfile.m, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\guide\guidemfile.mMATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: guidemfile.m.oldDownload the attached file, guidemfile.m storing it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Legacy Code Tool can generate incorrect C-MEX S-function and TLC block files
Description: The Legacy Code Tool can generate incorrect C-MEX S-function and TLC block files if both of the following conditions exist: An array of structure specifications is used for generating more than one C-MEX S-function with a single call to the legacy_code function One of the structure specifications sets the InitializeConditionsFcnSpec field to a string that names a reentrant function that the S-function is intended to call to initialize and reset states  ? ? ? ?
Workaround: Explicitly generate the C-MEX S-function and TLC block files inside a for loop instead of generating them with the vector syntax. For example:def1 = legacy_code('initialize');def1.SFunctionName = ... % fill the 1st LCT data structure ...def2 = legacy_code('initialize');def2.SFunctionName = ... % fill the 2nd LCT data structure ...def3 = legacy_code('initialize');def3.SFunctionName = ... % fill the 3rd LCT data structure ...defs = [def1;def2;def3];for k = 1:3,    legacy_code('sfcn_cmex_generate', defs(k));    legacy_code('sfcn_tlc_generate', defs(k));endlegacy_code('compile', defs);legacy_code('rtwmakecfg', defs);

Summary: A false compilation error may occur with homonymic primitive operations and pragma import
Description: If all of the following conditions are true: A tagged record type with a primitive operation Prim_Op  is derived A homonymic primitive operation with a pragma import (C, Prim_Op, "lib_name_of_prim_op")  is defined for the derived type The inherited primitive operation Prim_Op  is redefined for the derived type  then the following errors are given during the compilation:   Verifier found an error in [some location] : not subtype conformant with declaration at line ...
